# Local Environmental Issues

Innovative Recycling Programs in Schools: Bringing Sustainability to Education Education is not only about textbooks and exams; it's also about teaching the next generation the importance of sustainability and stewardship of the Earth. As of September 2023, numerous schools across the country have taken this lesson to heart by integrating innovative recycling programs into their curriculums and daily operations. In many cases, these programs have not only reduced waste but have also educated students on the importance of environmental conservation. The Rise of School-Based Recycling Initiatives The past few years have seen a steady rise in environmental awareness among young people, and schools have played a pivotal role in this shift. In October 2023, a survey by the National Education Association revealed that approximately 80% of schools now participate in some form of recycling or sustainability initiative. This is a significant increase from just a few years prior, underscoring the growing commitment to green practices within educational institutions. Recycling Programs Tailored to Young Minds Understanding that children and teenagers learn differently, schools have crafted their recycling programs to be both engaging and educational. The programs emphasize handson involvement and integrate interactive tools to make recycling a fun and thoughtprovoking part of the school day. These sophisticated programs often go beyond simple paper and plastic recycling, encouraging students to think critically about a variety of materials and their lifecycle. How Schools Are Incorporating Innovative Recycling Techniques One way that schools are standing out in their recycling efforts is by utilizing technology to make the process more interactive. Some schools have introduced apps that track the amount of materials their community recycles, providing students with tangible evidence of their contributions to the environment. Others have implemented reward systems where students earn points for recycling which can be exchanged for privileges or prizes, adding an element of competition and reward to the process. The Role of Students in Shaping the Recycling Culture Students aren't just participants in these initiatives; they are often at the forefront of their development and implementation. In December 2023, we witnessed students at Lincoln Middle School form a 'Green Club' responsible for the school's recycling program. These students hold regular meetings, plan recycling drives, and collaborate with local businesses to find innovative ways to recycle materials that are typically difficult to process, like certain plastics and electronic components. The Educational Impact of Recycling Programs The benefits of these recycling programs go well beyond environmental impact. They are a practical application of many academic concepts from various subjects. Science classes discuss the chemistry of decomposition and the impact of waste on ecosystems, while mathematics lessons can incorporate data analysis from the school's recycling figures. These interdisciplinary links help to solidify students' understanding and demonstrate the real-world application of their classroom learning. Involvement Beyond the Schoolyard An unexpected and beneficial outcome of these innovative school recycling programs has been the increased environmental awareness among families and communities. Students often become ambassadors for recycling at home, encouraging their families to participate and adopt greener habits. Furthermore, some schools collaborate with local governments and organizations to expand recycling options in their neighborhoods, thereby magnifying their impact. Securing a Green Future with Ongoing Education The innovative recycling programs in schools are not merely about managing waste; they represent a crucial investment in the future. By instilling a sense of responsibility and knowledge about sustainability in the younger generation, schools ensure that these practices continue to evolve and improve. As students educated in these programs grow up and enter various professions, they carry with them the lessons learned about environmental stewardship, spreading the ethos of sustainability across all sectors of society. Overcoming Challenges in School Recycling Programs Implementing and maintaining recycling programs isn't without its challenges. Funding is a primary concern, as these programs require investment in bins, educational materials, and sometimes even staffing. Additionally, the logistics of collecting and sorting recycled materials can be complex, particularly in larger schools or districts. To overcome these potential hurdles, schools like Jefferson High, under the guidance of Principal Angela Ramirez, have sought grants and formed partnerships with local waste management agencies. Such collaborations provide schools with the necessary resources and expertise to implement effective recycling programs and ensure that recycled materials are processed correctly. Measuring Success Through Feedback and Adaptation The effectiveness of these recycling programs often hinges on consistent evaluation and adaptation. Feedback from students, staff, and the community, alongside data on waste reduction and recycling rates, is invaluable for refining these initiatives. This iterative process allows schools to identify what works, what doesn't, and how to better motivate the school community towards positive environmental action. Future Perspectives on Recycling Education Looking forward to the rest of the 2023-2024 school year and beyond, the trend toward green education shows no signs of slowing. Schools continue to implement innovative programs, expanding upon existing recycling measures, and developing new ways to educate and involve their students in environmental stewardship. As these programs become an integral part of the school culture, they pave the way for a more informed and proactive approach to sustainability that can have lasting effects on the future health of our planet. In conclusion, the march towards a greener future is being led by the youngest among us, with schools playing a critical role in equipping students with the knowledge and habits necessary for a sustainable lifestyle. While there are certainly challenges to be faced, the innovative spirit displayed by students and educators alike provides hope that these programs will not only persist but continue to evolve and inspire environmental change. The final paragraph serves as an end-cap to the topic, though it does not summarize the article in the conventional sense of a conclusion.]

# Transfer Your Post-9/11 GI Bill Benefits | Veterans Affairs#1

Transfer your Post-9/11 GI Bill benefits Find out if you can transfer any of your unused Post-9/11 GI Bill benefits to your spouse or dependent children. Note : The Department of Defense DoD decides whether you can transfer GI Bill benefits to your family. Can I transfer benefits? You can transfer education benefits if you re on active duty or in the Selected Reserve and you meet the requirements listed below. At least one of these must be true : You have completed at least 6 years of service on the date your request is approved and you agree to add 4 more years of service , or You have completed at least 10 years of service on the date your request is approved, can t commit to add 4 more years of service because of either a policy or statute, but agree to serve for the maximum amount of time allowed And this must also be true : The person getting benefits has enrolled in the Defense Enrollment Eligibility Reporting System DEERS. Who s covered? Qualified dependents What benefits can my qualified dependents get? If the DoD approves the Transfer of Entitlement TOE , your spouse or dependent children can apply for up to 36 months of benefits, and may be able to get money for : Tuition Housing Books and supplies When can they use the transferred benefits? These conditions apply to family members using transferred benefits : Spouses May use the benefit right away May use the benefit while you re on active duty or after you ve separated from service Don t qualify for the monthly housing allowance while you re on active duty May use the benefit for up to 15 years after your separation from active duty Children May start to use the benefit only after you ve finished at least 10 years of service May use the benefit while you re on active duty or after you ve separated from service May not use the benefit until they ve gotten a high school diploma or equivalency certificate , or have reached 18 years of age Qualify for the monthly housing allowance even when you re on active duty Don t have to use the benefit within 15 years after your separation from active duty , but can t use the benefit after they ve turned 26 years old. Your dependents may still qualify even if a child marries or you and your spouse divorce. However , service members and Veterans can revoke cancel or change a TOE at any time. If you want to totally revoke transferred benefits for a dependent and you re still in the service , please turn in another transfer request for the dependent through milConnect. If a dependent s transfer eligibility ability to get a TOE has been totally revoked , you can t transfer benefits again to that dependent. How do I transfer the benefit? While you re still on active duty , you ll request to transfer, change, or revoke a Transfer of Entitlement TOE through milConnect. You can t apply for a TOE through us. Transfer , change , or revoke a TOE If the DoD approves the TOE , your family members may apply for benefits. Apply online now , or Apply by mail. Fill out and mail an Application for Family Member to Use Transferred Benefits VA Form 22 - 1990E to the nearest VA regional benefit office. Download VA Form 22 - 1990E PDF Find your nearest VA regional benefit office Once you leave active duty , you can still provide a future effective date for when the TOE can be used, change the number of months transferred, or revoke the TOE by submitting a written request to VA through milConnect. Get more information DoD website for information on transferring benefits Fact sheet on transferring Post-9/11 GI Bill benefits PDF To find out if you qualify, the status of your transfer request, and how to use the TEB Portlet for service - specific questions , please contact the right career counselor or personnel center from this list : Army Active - Duty Officer Email Army Active - Duty Enlisted Email Army National Guard Email Army Reserve Enlisted and Officer Email Navy Active - Duty Personnel 866-U - ASK - NPC 866 - 827 - 5672 or DSN 882 - 5672 Navy Reserve 800 - 621 - 8853 , Fax : 757 - 444 - 7597/7598 Email Marine Corps Active - Duty Officer Email Marine Corps Active - Duty Enlisted Email Marine Corps Reserve Email Air Force Active Duty 800 - 525 - 0102 or 210 - 565 - 5000 or DSN 665 - 5000 Air National Guard Contact unit retention manager Air Force Reserve Email Coast Guard Active Duty Email Coast Guard Reserve Email NOAA Email or 301 - 713 - 7728 PHS Email or 240 - 453 - 6130

# New York State Insurance Requirements#3

Do I need insurance? Yes. To register a vehicle in New York State you must have New York State issued automobile liability insurance coverage. If you do not maintain the coverage , the DMV can suspend your vehicle registration and your driver license. What kind of insurance do I need? New York law requires that you have auto liability insurance coverage. The minimum amount of liability coverage is $ 10,000 for property damage for a single accident $ 25,000 for bodily injury and $50,000 for death for a person involved in an accident $ 50,000 for bodily injury and $100,000 for death for two or more people in an accident Your liability insurance coverage must while the registration is valid, even if you don t use the vehicle there are exceptions for motorcycles 1 remain in effect be New York State insurance coverage , issued by a company licensed by the NY State Department of Financial Services and certified by NY State DMV - out - of - state insurance is never acceptable be issued in the name of the vehicle registrant, and remain in the name of the registrant at all times 2 The New York State Department of Financial Services website has more information about liability insurance and insurance companies licensed in New York: http://www.dfs.ny.gov [1]. More than 2 names on a vehicle registration There can only be 1 or 2 names on a registration registrants. Both the primary registrant first name listed on the registration and the co - registrant must sign the Vehicle Registration / Title Application PDF [2] MV-82 and provide their proofs of identity and date of birth. Both names must appear on the Insurance ID Card. Do I need insurance to register my vehicle? Yes. The DMV requires auto liability insurance to register a vehicle in New York. When you get insurance , your insurance company will issue proof of insurance in two ways. It will give you two original NY State Insurance ID Cards see a sample [3] or provide you with access to your digital electronic NY State Insurance ID Card 3 send an electronic notice of insurance coverage to the DMV your insurance agent or broker cannot file this notice Your NY State Insurance Identification Cards and the electronic notice of insurance together verify your insurance coverage. We need both. You must register your vehicle at the DMV within 180 days of the effective date on your insurance ID card. Bring one copy or form of your Insurance Identification Cards with you. The DMV office will keep the paper card. Keep the other paper card with the vehicle as your proof of insurance. Anyone operating your vehicle must be able to provide proof of insurance while they are operating the vehicle. Does the name on my vehicle registration application and Insurance Identification Card need to be the same? Yes. Your insurance cards must have the same name as the name on your vehicle registration application. Will the DMV accept copies or faxes of my Insurance Identification Card? Yes. However , the DMV will not accept a card if the DMV barcode reader cannot read scan the barcode. Out-of-state insurance documents We will never accept out - of - state vehicle insurance coverage of any type. If your vehicle is registered in New York , it must have New York State auto liability insurance coverage. Your motorcycle must have insurance coverage any time it is operated on a public road or highway. Unlike most other motor vehicles, motorcycles are registered for one year rather than two , and all motorcycle registrations expire on April 30. Also , you can terminate your motorcycle liability insurance without surrendering the plate at a DMV office. This makes it easier to put the motorcycle back on the road after winter storage. However , under no circumstances may your motorcycle operate on public highways without liability coverage in effect. A change on the insurance policy to a name different from the name on your registration may cause a lapse in insurance coverage , and DMV may suspend your driver license and your vehicle registration. Your vehicle's insurance and registration must always be in exactly the same name. You should always carry the paper or access to your digital electronic NY State Insurance ID Card in your vehicle. # Local News

The Dawn of Green Neighborhoods: Eco-Friendly Infrastructure Projects Take Root As communities worldwide grapple with the pressing issue of climate change, one local initiative stands out for its proactive stance on environmental sustainability. The town of Evergreen Valley has recently launched a series of infrastructure projects aimed at creating a more eco-friendly environment. Through a concerted effort that began in earnest on September 8, 2023, municipal managers in collaboration with sustainability experts like Dr. Linda Ferguson and urban planner Marcus Juarez, have set into motion a plan that intends to transform their neighborhood into a green utopia by 2030. Understanding the Essence of Eco-Friendly Projects To comprehend the full scope of the undertaking, it's imperative to first understand what defines eco-friendly infrastructure. These are construction projects and urban planning initiatives that prioritize sustainability, often by reducing environmental impact, incorporating renewable resources, utilizing energy-efficient designs, and promoting the conservation of natural habitats. Strategic Planning and Community Involvement Key to this endeavor has been a meticulous planning phase which started in late September, where urban planners and environmental scientists came together to form a comprehensive strategy. Public forums, headed by Dr. Ferguson, who holds a PhD in Environmental Science, and Juarez, were conducted to educate residents and solicit their input. The town's residents, who have seen the consequences of unchecked development, were quick to get involved. Homeowners like 58-year-old Maria Vasquez, transformed from passive observers to active participants after attending these forums. "I realized we all have a role to play. The choices we make now decide the future of our children," she shared with passion. Promoting Renewable Energy Infrastructure One of the first implementations was the installation of solar panels on public buildings, which began on November 15, 2023. This initiative not only aims to reduce the carbon footprint of these structures but also serves as a testament to the town's commitment to renewable energy. By harnessing the power of the sun, these buildings have reduced dependence on fossil fuels, an effort spearheaded by the town's Energy Committee chairperson, Jonathan Green. Green Transportation Systems Evergreen Valley did not stop there. Developing an eco-friendly transportation network became the town's next target. By January 5, 2024, the town council had approved plans for a new bike share program and had dedicated more lanes for bicycles and electric scooters. The vision included the gradual transition to electric buses for public transportation, an initiative that Juarez argued would significantly cut down on vehicular emissions. Water Conservation and Management Understanding that water is a precious resource, the neighborhood turned its attention to implementing a state-of-the-art rainwater collection and recycling system in the public park. Officially inaugurated on March 22, 2024, coinciding with World Water Day, this system collects rainwater to irrigate the park and other green spaces, minimizing the amount of treated water used for these purposes. Building a Sustainable Food System The implementation of community gardens and support for urban agriculture is another element of the neighborhood's green transformation. Beginning in the spring of 2024, vacant lots were converted into gardens where residents could grow fruits and vegetables. This initiative not only provides access to fresh produce but also promotes local biodiversity and helps lower the carbon footprint associated with food transportation. Energy-Efficient Buildings Efforts to retrofit and remodel existing buildings to make them more energy-efficient were also set into motion. The town stipulated that all new construction must meet specific “green” criteria, including better insulation, natural lighting, and the installation of energyefficient appliances as a part of their building code as of May 1, 2024. Preservation and Expansion of Green Spaces Recognizing the value of green spaces in urban environments, Evergreen Valley has made it a priority to preserve existing parks and to create new ones. The establishment of these areas not only contributes to the aesthetic value of the neighborhood but also serves an ecological purpose by providing habitats for local wildlife and improving air quality. Addressing Waste Management The adoption of a comprehensive recycling program was launched on July 10, 2024, which expanded beyond traditional household waste to include electronic waste and composting. Through educational campaigns and community workshops, the project leaders have been increasing the awareness about the importance of recycling and proper waste disposal. Integrating Smart Technology With an eye on the future, the planners incorporated smart technology into the infrastructure to foster a more interconnected and efficient community. From smart street lighting that adjusts to save energy to sensors that monitor air and water quality, the investment in technology is a testament to the town's dedication to sustainability. Quantifying the Impact The science behind these eco-friendly measures points to broader impacts. A reduction in carbon emissions is expected to be quantifiable within the next few years, as is a decrease in water and energy consumption. The psychological impact is also being studied, with early evidence suggesting that greener neighborhoods contribute to lower stress levels and higher overall satisfaction among residents. Evergreen Valley represents just one example of how municipal planners and community members can come together to effect environmental change. The challenges facing global sustainability are sizable, but through initiatives like these, the blueprint for a greener tomorrow is being carefully drafted. With careful monitoring and ongoing community engagement, Evergreen Valley is setting a precedent for what's possible when a neighborhood embraces the ethos of eco-friendliness and works collectively to implement it.

# Incident Report

Maintenance Issues Leading to Service Interruptions in Urban Transit In the ever-evolving landscape of urban transit, cities around the globe work tirelessly to maintain optimal service for their bustling populace. However, maintenance issues sometimes lead to unforeseen disruptions, leaving commuters scrambling for alternatives. This deep dive looks into a notable instance that occurred on October 17, 2023, when the city of Metropoliville faced a cascade of service interruptions across its transit system. The Day the Rails Stood Still On a brisk fall morning, at precisely 8:12 AM, the Metropolitan Transit Authority (MTA) of Metropoliville announced that the Blue Line subway would experience significant delays due to an unexpected maintenance issue. As workers and students began their daily routines, this revelation came as an unwelcome surprise to thousands who relied on the city’s swiftest transit artery. Johnathan Hargrove, a regular commuter and an IT specialist, voiced his frustration. "It threw my entire day off. Normally, it's a smooth 20-minute ride to the office, but that day, it took me nearly an hour and a half to get to work," he recalled. Unpacking the Maintenance Woes Investigations post-outage revealed that the interruption was due to a power supply malfunction in the central signaling system. To understand the intricacy of this issue, the MTA's Chief Maintenance Officer, Dr. Susan Park, explained, "The signaling system is the nervous system of our transit network. A glitch in power can compromise the safety of our operations, necessitating an immediate halt to service." What may appear as a simple inconvenience on the surface is an intricate ballet of maintaining aging infrastructure within Metropolitan cities. Metropoliville's Blue Line, now nearly 60 years old, is a testament to the urban struggle against time and usage. Community Impact and the Ripple Effect The consequence of this service outage was felt citywide. Retailer Mike Bronson pointed out that the slowdown wasn't just on the transit lines but also in the day's earnings. "When people can't get here, they don't shop here. It's as simple as that," he said, noting a noticeable dip in morning business. Additionally, schools in the area reported tardiness, with some students missing important assessments. Marsha Elliot, a high school teacher, highlighted the wider implications. "It's not just about being late. It disrupts the learning flow for the whole day. Students are stressed, and the planned curriculum gets pushed back." Emergency Response and Alternative Measures While the transit authority faced backlash for the outage, its rapid response was a notable point of fiscal importance. Shuttle buses were swiftly deployed, and updates were funneled through the MTA’s app and social media pages. Gerald Watkins, the operations manager responsible for coordinating the response, emphasized the importance of a contingency plan. "Our job is to ensure that when these situations arise—and they will—we're ready with an alternative," he stated proudly. However, despite these efforts, the capacity discrepancy between a full-scale train service and a hastily assembled bus convoy was glaring. The Accountability of the Transit Authority Following the outage, civic leaders and consumer advocacy groups demanded accountability and a detailed review of the MTA's maintenance regimen. At a town hall meeting on November 6, 2023, MTA executives faced the public to articulate their commitment to improving maintenance protocols. "We're not just finding band-aid solutions," declared MTA CEO, Darren Thompson. He unveiled a multi-million-dollar initiative aimed at overhauling the Blue Line's aging infrastructure with state-of-the-art technology designed to preempt and swiftly rectify such incidents. Economic Implications of Disrupted Commutes Research economist Laura Jensen highlighted that service outages have greater economic repercussions. "It’s not just about the cost of repairs or lost revenue for businesses. There's an opportunity cost - the economic activities that didn't happen because people were stuck in transit," Jensen elucidated. These disruptions can add up, especially in a bustling metropolis with a high reliance on public transportation. Forward-Looking: Investing in Preventative Maintenance It became abundantly clear post-outage that preventative maintenance should be at the forefront of transit discussions. Forward-thinking cities are learning from instances like Metropoliville's snafu, understanding that investing in maintenance can vastly reduce the potential for severe service disruptions. The MTA of Metropoliville accepted this as it pledged an increase in its maintenance budget for the forthcoming fiscal year, with a keen focus on the incorporation of predictive analytics. Technological Innovations to Mitigate Risks In an era where data is king, the transit industry is increasingly turning to technological solutions. Predictive maintenance, powered by machine learning and artificial intelligence, could be a game-changer in preempting issues before they become outages. Software engineer and urban planning consultant, Rajiv Malhotra, weighed in, "We're looking at systems that can analyze patterns and predict equipment failures. It's about staying one step ahead." With such advancements, the MTA and other transit authorities could very well transition from reactive strategies to proactive ones. Training the Workforce for a More Resilient Transit System Finally, industry experts underscore the urgent need for well-trained maintenance staff, equipped with the technical know-how to deal with advanced infrastructures. Educational programs focusing on modern urban systems and their care are thus becoming more critical than ever in shaping a robust and reliable workforce for the transit of tomorrow. As urban landscapes continue to grow and the reliance on public transit intensifies, maintenance issues such as the ones experienced by Metropoliville will undeniably recur. It stands to reason that as transit networks become more complex, the solutions to maintaining them must evolve correspondingly. It is through an amalgamation of investment, innovation, and training that city transit systems can aspire to minimize outages and truly thrive in serving their communities.

# Veteran status designation on a photo document#3

If you are a qualified U.S. military veteran who was honorably discharged , and you have a NY State driver license, learner permit or non - driver photo ID card , you can have the word " Veteran " printed on the upper left hand of your photo document . There is no additional fee for the veteran status designation on your document , but all regular transaction fees will apply. After you receive the veteran designation , it will automatically be on all of your future DMV documents when you replace or renew. To apply, you must provide proof of an honorable discharge Acceptable proofs are that indicates an honorable discharge a U.S. Department of Defense Form DD-214 that indicates an honorable discharge a form DD-215 if discharged before January 1,1950 , an Honorable Discharge form WD AGO 53, WD AGO 55, WD AGO 53 - 55, NAVPERS 553, NAVMC 78PD or NAVCG 553, or an " Enlisted Separation Paper " a Certification of Military Service NA FORM 13038 that indicates a discharge that is either " honorable " or " under honorable conditions " * If your license or permit was issued before October 30 , 2017 and is not Enhanced , you have a Standard document. * * Boarding a domestic U.S. flight , and entering a Federal Building or Military Base starting October , 2020 * * * U.S. Border Crossing from Canada, Mexico and some Caribbean countries Learn more about Enhanced, REAL ID and Standard documents [1]. Apply at DMV office if you also want to change to an Enhanced or REAL ID If you have a Standard license and want to change to a REAL ID or Enhanced while adding your veteran status , you must come into a DMV office. Complete this guide to make sure you have the proofs of identification you need. You will also need to bring your proof of honorable discharge original or photocopies. Find your proofs [2 ] By mail Send a completed Application for Permit , Driver License or Non - Driver ID Card pdf [3] MV-44 your proof of honorable discharge send photocopies, your original documents DMV License Production Bureau Room 331 , 6 Empire State Plaza Albany, NY 12228 You cannot apply by phone or online. If you applied to have the Veteran Indicator added to your license, permit or non - driver ID and were charged a document fee , you are entitled to a refund for the fee A law has been enacted that permits us to refund a fee paid by a veteran in order to place a veteran indicator on their DMV document. You don't need to request a refund or visit a DMV office. Each eligible customer will get a letter and refund check from New York State.

# Register an ATV#1

Register an ATV[1] What is considered an all-terrain vehicle (ATV )? An all - terrain vehicle ATV is any self - propelled vehicle, including an off - road motorcycle, that is manufactured for use on off - road trails or in off - road competitions. These vehicles are less than 70 inches wide and weigh less than 1,000 pounds. For information , see our brochure ATVs: Information for Owners and Operators [2] C-29. Does an ATV need a vehicle registration? Yes. You must have a valid NY State registration to ride your ATV in New York State, unless it is only used for agricultural purposes or not - for - hire snow removal on private property registered in another state to a resident of that state only used at special events competitions If you purchase an ATV including an off - road motorcycle from a NY State ATV dealer , the dealer is required to register it before it is delivered to you. What do I need to bring to the DMV? To register an ATV not purchased from a NY State ATV dealer , please complete the Document Guide to make sure you bring the correct documents with you to the DMV. Find your proofs [3 ] Summary of what you need acceptable proof of ownership see information below a completed Registration / Title Application PDF [4] MV-82 that the NY State sales tax was paid or proof of the purchase price of the ATV 1 proof proof of identity 2 cash , check , money order or credit / debit card to pay for fees For quicker service , make a reservation online before visiting offices in New York City, Westchester, Nassau, Suffolk, Rockland, Onondaga, Monroe or Albany Counties. For all other counties , see our office locations and hours [5]. Make a Reservation [6 ] What are acceptable proofs of ownership? If you purchase the ATV from an out - of - state ATV dealer , acceptable proof of ownership can be a manufacturer's certificate of origin or a manufacturer's statement of origin , and the bill of sale from the dealer the registration or the title certificate signed by the previous owner that transfers the ownership to the dealer , and a bill of sale from the dealer that transfers the ownership to you A bill of sale from an ATV dealer must include the name and the address of the dealer and the registration number of the dealer your name and your address the Vehicle Identification Number VIN of the ATV the year , the make, the model , and the number of wheels of the ATV that the ATV is either used or new an indication that the dealer collected the correct NY State sales tax an indication If the ATV is transferred by a private individual , acceptable proof of ownership is a New York State or out - of - state transferable registration or title certificate signed by the previous owner to transfer ownership to you If the current owner did not register the ATV , a transferrable registration and bill of sale that transferred ownership to the current owner , and a bill of sale that transfers ownership to you. Do I need insurance coverage for my ATV? You don t need insurance to register your ATV. However , you must have insurance unless you only drive your ATV on private property. You must show your proof of insurance to a judge, a police officer, or any person who makes a claim of injury or property damage. Do I need a driver license to drive an ATV? You do not need a driver license to drive an ATV , but restrictions apply to ATV drivers under the age of 16. Children 10 to 15 years of age can drive an ATV only with adult supervision without adult supervision on property where their parent or guardian is the owner or tenant without adult supervision on property where ATV use is permitted and the child completed an ATV safety - training course approved by the DMV carry your course certificate with you when you drive Children under the age of 10 may drive an ATV only with adult supervision without adult supervision on property where their parent or guardian is the owner or tenant You can operate an ATV on public lands where signs indicate that ATV use is allowed. Only operate an ATV on private property if you have permission from the property owner. If you purchased the ATV from an out - of - state ATV dealer , and the bill of sale indicates that the dealer collected the NY State sales tax , no additional sales tax is due. If the out - of - state dealer collects out - of - state sales tax , you can apply for credit toward the payment of the NY State sales tax when you apply for registration in NY State. You can be eligible for credit only if New York and the other state have a reciprocal sales tax agreement see Sales Tax Paid in Another State {Sales Tax Form} PDF [7] DTF-804. If you purchased the ATV from a private seller , the seller and the buyer complete the Statement of Transaction {Sales Tax Form} PDF [8] DTF-802. You can also show your proof of exemption or proof that the tax was paid. A NY State Driver License, Learner Permit, or Non - Driver ID card that is current or not expired for more than 2 years can serve as proof of identity. If you don t have one of these documents , you must provide other kinds of proof [9]. Show "do it online" button in megamenu: Topic: Registration [10 ] Related PDFs: show mydmv button in megamenu: Disable this transaction?: yes or no survey:

# Eligibility Requirements For VA Home Loan Programs | Veterans Affairs#1

Eligibility requirements for VA home loan programs Learn about VA home loan eligibility requirements for a VA direct or VA - backed loan. Find out how to apply for a Certificate of Eligibility COE to show your lender that you qualify based on your service history and duty status. Keep in mind that for a VA - backed home loan, you ll also need to meet your lender s credit and income loan requirements to receive financing. Can I get a Certificate of Eligibility for a VA direct or VA-backed home loan? You may be able to get a COE if you didn t receive a dishonorable discharge and you meet the minimum active - duty service requirement based on when you served. How do I apply for my COE? You can apply online right now. Go to eBenefits to apply Learn more about how to apply for your COE What if I don t meet the minimum service requirements? You may still be able to get a COE if you were discharged for one of the reasons listed below. You must have been discharged for one of these reasons : Hardship , or The convenience of the government , or Reduction in force , or Certain medical conditions , or A service - connected disability a disability related to your military service What should I do if I received an other than honorable, bad conduct, or dishonorable discharge? If you ve received one of these discharge statuses , you may not be eligible for VA benefits. There are 2 ways you can try to qualify : Find out how to apply for a discharge upgradeLearn about the VA Character of Discharge review process Can I get a COE as the spouse of a Veteran? You may be able to get a COE if you re the surviving spouse of a Veteran or the spouse of a Veteran who is missing in action or being held as a Prisoner of War POW. Find out how to apply for a COE as a surviving spouse Can I get a COE in any other situations? You may be able to get a COE if you meet at least one of the requirements listed below. At least one of these must be true. You: Are a U.S. citizen who served in the Armed Forces of a government allied with the United States in World War II , or Served as a member in certain organizations, like a : Public Health Service officer Cadet at the United States Military, Air Force, or Coast Guard Academy Midshipman at the United States Naval Academy Officer of the National Oceanic & Atmospheric Administration Merchant seaman during World War II Can I use a COE I used before? You may be able to restore an entitlement you used in the past to buy another home with a VA direct or VA - backed loan if you meet at least one of the requirements listed below. At least one of these must be true : You ve sold the home you bought with the prior loan and have paid that loan in full , or A qualified Veteran - transferee agrees to assume your loan and substitute their entitlement for the same amount of entitlement you used originally , or You ve repaid your prior loan in full , but haven t sold the home you bought with that loan you can only do this one time To request an entitlement restoration , fill out a Request for a Certificate of Eligibility VA Form 26 - 1880 and send it to the VA regional loan center for your state. Download VA Form 26 - 1880Find your state s VA regional loan center

# Checklists for Academic and Financial Preparation | Federal Student Aid#1

Are you a student? A parent? An adult thinking about going back to school? Want to know how to prepare for college or career school? We ve provided checklists with the steps you can take to help your or your child s chances of getting into and being able to afford the school of your choice. Students , college prep is about more than the classes that you take. It s also about developing the skills that will help you succeed in college and life. For instance , as you balance studying and having fun , you ll be practicing time management ; and as you save for college, you ll be learning money management. Our checklists suggest these and many other steps you can take, as well as websites you can explore, as you prepare academically and financially for college. Parents , each of the checklists from elementary school through high school includes items for you, such as how to support your child in his or her studies, where to set up a college savings account, and what to do when it s time to apply for financial aid. Adult students , we ve got an adult student checklist just for you so that you can skip all the information about what classes to take in high school and get straight to exactly what you need. Try This Resource College Preparation Checklist Helps students elementary, middle, and high school, as well as adults and parents prepare financially and academically for college. College Preparation YouTube Playlist Curious about what college is like? Take a look at the videos in our Prepare for College " YouTube playlist. The playlist includes such topics as What s the biggest myth about college? and What s the best tip about going to college? What is the Most Surprising Thing About College? Checklists Whether you re a student or parent, years away from college or just about to start , the following checklists will help you get ready. Elementary School Checklist : Student and parent checklists that start the student on the road to enjoying learning and point the parent to resources for college savings accounts. Middle School Checklist : Student and parent checklists that get the student thinking about high school and possible careers and encourage the parent to keep an eye on the student s progress. High School Checklist : Student and parent checklists that help the student focus on succeeding academically and learning about financial aid and provide the parent with tips for supporting the student and participating in the financial aid application process. 9th Grade Checklist 10th Grade Checklist 11th Grade Checklist 12th Grade Checklist Grad School Checklist : Checklist to help individuals get prepared for graduate or professional school. Adult Student Checklist : Checklist for adults applying to college , including those who left high school before graduating, graduated high school, completed some college courses, or may be in the workforce. Getting a Late Start? Last - minute Checklist : Checklist for anyone who has been accepted at a college and is starting classes soon but hasn t applied for financial aid yet.

# Local Technology and Innovation

Navigating Funding Rounds: From Seed to Success As a tech startup founder, I've walked the challenging and often exhilarating path of funding rounds. I've felt the uncertainties, celebrated the wins, and learned from the setbacks. In this personal account, I'd like to share with you the intimate journey of how a startup, which began as a mere idea over a casual coffee back in mid-September 2023, has navigated through the complex terrain of funding to reach a place of sustainable growth and innovation. The Spark of Beginnings: The Seed Stage Our journey began with an idea—a solution to a problem we spotted in the ever-evolving realm of remote collaboration. Immediately realizing the gravity of what we had, we set out to transform this concept into a viable business. But to do so, we needed capital, and that's where the seed funding came into play. I remember our first pitch—it was on the 15th of October, 2023, in a small conference room that we had booked for the occasion, facing an audience of potential angel investors. Samira Khan was one of them, an established entrepreneur known for her sharp intuition and a keen eye for promising startups. The pitch went by in a whirlwind of hopes, meticulously crafted slide decks, and elevated heart rates. But at the end, it wasn't the slides that sealed the deal—it was the passion and belief we communicated. Seed funding, it turned out, was not just about the idea or the potential for profitability; it was about trust in the team. The Leap into the Market: Accelerators and Incubators With seed money in our bank, we aimed for growth—rapid and tangible. We were selected for a technology incubator, ThinkTech Accelerate, which commenced on January 5, 2024. This three-month program was crucial. It provided us with mentorship from industry veterans, introduced us to a network of fellow tech entrepreneurs, and, importantly, helped us fine-tune our business model. In these accelerator programs, we were not just building a product; we were sculpting a company culture, refining our business acumen, and preparing for the rigorous demands of the market. One mentor, James O'Connor, always reiterated, "Your startup is more than your product—it's your vision made manifest." Those words became a guiding mantra as we prepared for the next funding phase. Series A: Scaling Up Emboldened by our progress and the validation from ThinkTech Accelerate, we plunged into the Series A funding round in late June 2024. With a prototype in hand and early adopters on board, we were no longer a speculative venture—our startup was a reality with metrics to prove it. I reached out to venture capital firms, but it was a chance encounter with Emily Torres, a venture capitalist renowned for backing leading-edge tech ventures, that accelerated our journey. Emily's firm showed keen interest and decided to lead our Series A with a hefty investment of $5 million. The pressure was intense; we had to demonstrate significant growth potentials, a scalable business model, and a path to profitability. The due diligence was rigorous, and every forecast was scrutinized, but we emerged from the process with a clarity of purpose and a much-needed capital injection to scale up operations. The Growth Trajectory: Series B and Beyond As 2025 dawned, our startup had grown considerably. We had a solid user base, consistent revenue streams, and a team that doubled in size. Now, it was time to expand further—to widen our market reach, enrich our product offerings, and reinforce our infrastructure. Series B was a starkly different experience. Strategies that had charmed our seed investors and early VCs weren't enough now. It was all about hard numbers, user acquisition costs, lifetime value, churn rates, and EBITDA forecasts. It was during this round, dated April 2, 2025, that Darius Moretti, of Moretti Ventures, gave us the tough love we needed. He pointed out areas where our efficiency could improve and strategies that we could adopt to profitability that we had not yet considered. He was instrumental in not only funding our Series B but in shaping the strategic direction that would take us to new heights. Our Series B ended with an investment summit on July 23, 2025. That day, we secured $15 million, a testament to our track record and the confidence investors had in our future. This infusion of capital was earmarked for international expansion and product development, positioning us as a leader in our niche of the tech industry. The Nascent Phase of IPO Preparations With our Series C on the horizon and whispers of an initial public offering (IPO) beginning to circulate among the board and investors, the air is thick with anticipation and daunting responsibilities. We are now working with investment banks, finalizing our strategies, and preparing our company to meet the stringent compliance requirements of going public. The prospect of an IPO is both exhilarating and grounding. The thought of taking the company I started over a casual coffee to the floors of the stock exchange fills me with indescribable pride. Yet, it's a phase replete with meticulous preparation, an evolving financial narrative, and a deep commitment to transparency and corporate governance. Navigating the roller coaster of startup funding isn’t for the faint-hearted, nor is it a oneperson endeavor. It's a symphony of collective effort, a nexus where the vision of founders meets the prowess of investors, mentors, and employees. It's a testament to persistence, adaptation, and unwavering belief in a dream. As I continue this journey, I can't predict where the road will end or how the landscape will evolve with each passing milestone. But one thing is certain: it's a ride filled with learning, growth, and the unyielding spirit of innovation that propels us forward to create, disrupt, and transform the technology landscape.

# News Stories

Sky Quakes: The Invisible Shattering Force Imagine a crisp autumn morning, and you’re out enjoying the serenity of nature. Suddenly, the tranquility is broken by a loud boom, akin to distant thunder, but the skies are clear. You’ve just encountered what's termed a "sky quake," an auditory phenomenon that has left both citizens and scientists perplexed. The Phenomenon of Sky Quakes Explained Sky quakes, or mystery booms, are enigmatic events that have been reported around the world with increasing frequency since late 2023. Descriptions vary from massive sonic booms to the rumblings akin to underground explosions, yet they happen in clear skies and often in locations devoid of any military or industrial activity. The sounds are jarring enough to shake buildings and rattle windows, sowing a mix of wonder and concern amongst the populace. On September 15, 2023, the small town of Eerieborough was startled by such an event. Local resident, Jessica Milner, recalls, “It was like the whole sky was cracking open. You could feel the vibration in your chest. It's nothing like I’ve ever experienced before.” These occurrences have mobilized scientists to delve deeper into the potential causes of these unsettling noises. Geological and Atmospheric Conditions Leading to Sky Quakes Experts have begun to draw connections between sky quakes and certain geological and atmospheric conditions. Dr. Harrison Lufer, a geophysicist specializing in atmospheric sciences at the CalTech University, provides a scholarly analysis: “There are many theories, but a prevalent thought is that these occurrences might be tied to sudden releases of deep earth gases, thermal inversions in the atmosphere, or even distant seismic activities.” A thermal inversion, where a layer of warm air sits over a layer of cooler air, can act like a refractive lens for sound waves. Sounds originating from far away, such as explosions or jet engines, can be carried over long distances and appear to come from directly overhead. Recent Studies and Theories Recent studies, some initiated after the surge of incidents following September 2023, have canvassed a broad range of possible causes for sky quakes. Seismic activity, it turns out, can create audible effects when the Earth's crust experiences sudden stress release. However, this does not account for all recorded incidents, as many have occurred in seismically inactive regions. Meteorologists like Dr. Nina Patel have put forth meteor-based theories, where small meteors that explode in the atmosphere could be the culprits behind some occurrences. On November 11, 2023, a research team led by Dr. Patel observed a sky quake in conjunction with a bolide tracking over the Pacific Northwest, suggesting a direct correlation with meteoric activity. Other researchers have turned their attention to the mysterious realms of our own planet's atmosphere. Phenomena such as "Seneca Guns" – named after the booms heard near Seneca Lake in upstate New York – and the "Moodus Noises" of Connecticut have historical records dating back centuries and may offer clues. These noises have often been attributed to minor earthquakes or collapses within the numerous small cavities that exist underground. However, the sporadic nature of sky quakes renders the investigation complex and ongoing. The Human Perception and Response Beneath the academic analysis lies the human response to these phenomena. Striking fear and awe alike, sky quakes have stirred public imagination and given rise to various interpretations. Some look upon these events with a supernatural lens, while others are motivated to seek out rational explanations. Social media has been a hotspot for discussions and speculations following each event. In particular, on December 19, 2023, after a particularly loud sky quake over the coastal city of Harborfield, social media was abuzz with videos, personal testimonials, and a plethora of theories attempting to explain the occurrence. The Journey toward Understanding Sky Quakes As researchers deepen their understanding, there’s a concurrent effort to demystify these events for the public. Dr. Lufer has been an advocate for educational outreach, holding town hall meetings in the aftermath of sky quakes. In such meetings, community members, including Jessica Milner from Eerieborough, have had the opportunity to converse with experts, discussing what they've experienced and learning about the scientific efforts to explain these events. The scientific community is adamant that the solution to the mystery of sky quakes will require interdisciplinary collaboration. From geologists and atmospheric scientists to acoustics specialists and seismologists, experts are pooling their knowledge to uncover the truth behind the phenomena. The Future of Sky Quake Research Looking forward, sky quake research is bound to expand, incorporating next-generation technologies like high-sensitivity barometers, infrasound recording equipment, and satellite surveillance. The fusion of these technologies may finally demystify the invisible shattering force that captivates and terrifies. In January 2024, the International Geological and Atmospheric Research Summit will convene experts from around the globe to discuss natural phenomena, including sky quakes. The Summit aims to establish a more cohesive global monitoring system, which can prompt timely alerts and foster a better understanding of these baffling atmospheric events. As the quest for answers continues, our knowledge of sky quakes remains in its infancy. With each recorded event, the scientific community builds upon the anecdotal evidence, hoping to piece together the puzzle. The phenomena of sky quakes exemplify the enduring human desire to understand the mysterious forces of nature, where each boom and rumble echo with the potential for discovery.

# Local News

Halifax City Council Marks the Beginning of Infrastructure Reinvestment In Halifax Halifax is poised for an infrastructure transformation following a string of ambitious decisions taken by its City Council recently. Guided by an innovative approach to sustainable urban planning, Halifax now stands on the precipice of an infrastructure revolution as its Council unveiled an expansive plan to upgrade roads, bridges, and public buildings across its region by September 2023 - which could cement Halifax as a model of contemporary urban development. The Blueprint for Change: Navigating the Path Ahead On October 15, the City Council made history when they approved a resolution setting aside substantial funds for the rehabilitation of outdated roads. Bayers Road will receive particular attention with plans for wider lanes to accommodate increased traffic as well as dedicated bike lanes and pedestrian pathways being added. Councilor Maria Gonzalez of Infrastructure Committee stated, "Our aim is not just refurbishing this road; rather we seek to transform it into an inclusive space that serves everyone from drivers, cyclists, and walkers alike. Another big-ticket project set to break ground this November is Halifax's Green Overpass project, featuring greenery that serves both as an essential connector between rapidly developing suburbs as well as serving as a living symbol of sustainability in its design. Energy Efficient Public Transit Solutions: Connecting Communities Together Public transport will receive an upgrade thanks to upgrades proposed for bus depots and the introduction of an eco-friendly bus fleet, led by Councilor Johnathan Lee. At a council meeting held September 20, Lee announced: "We're bringing the future of public transportation to Halifax!" These buses not only carry people around; they stand as mobile symbols of our commitment to making Halifax greener! Lee projects completion date is mid2024. Work will soon commence on a light rail system to offer faster and more efficient travel for residents in Halifax. Proponents such as Councilor Linda Singh believe it will foster stronger community ties while creating faster transit solutions. Public Buildings Get Revamp Halifax's public infrastructure, long due a facelift, is also up for revitalization. Central Library renovations are scheduled to commence early 2024 with renovations focused on creating dynamic multipurpose spaces using natural light while using energy-efficient systems. Miguel Hernandez of Miguel Hernandez Architecture's redesign project stated: "We envision a library that serves more than simply borrowing books; instead it serves as a hub of learning collaboration and community that operates at the cutting edge of energy efficiency design. City Hall, an historic landmark, will undergo restoration work that will preserve its heritage while modernizing internal systems to meet contemporary environmental standards. "Respecting our architectural legacy doesn't mean forgoing innovation; through these renovations we aim to lead by example in finding an equilibrium between history and progress," stated Emma Richards of the Heritage Committee chair. Connecting Tomorrow - Building Bridges Now Council deliberations has highlighted the innovation behind Honore Bridge. This state-ofthe-art bridge not only connects commercial district to East Halifax but is designed as an exhibit for modern engineering and sustainable design practices, including solar collection systems and eco-friendly materials used for its construction - showing Halifax's commitment to creating a cleaner and more resilient future. Once all regulatory hurdles have been cleared away, construction should commence early 2024. Council member Andre Dupont expressed great enthusiasm for the Honore Bridge as an infrastructure project which represents our city values. "This bridge stands as proof of what can be accomplished when technology meets vision," he said. Build Your Digital City An integral component of Halifax City Council's ambitious plan is upgrading Halifax's digital apparatus to smart city standards. Plans are in the works to integrate intelligent traffic systems and public Wi-Fi zones within existing framework. Susan Cho, Head of Digital Infrastructure Taskforce unveiled smart street-light project: high efficiency LED lights networked with sensors will moderate lighting based on pedestrian and vehicular traffic levels. "This initiative isn't simply about saving energy; it's also about beautifying and functionalising our city while making it safer and more livable," Cho added. Halifax's Urban Forest Strategy A Green Canopy: Halifax Urban Forest Initiative rounded off the list of projects on December 6, when it was passed unanimously by council. While its broad scope includes new parklands and tree planting throughout urban areas, its cornerstone feature is its integration of green spaces into new infrastructure layouts to form a 'green canopy' over the city. Environmental Planner Alice Munroe highlighted this impactful achievement by noting "We aren't simply planting trees; we are building a legacy: one that celebrates natural beauty alongside economic and cultural vibrancy". Halifax City Council's recent decisions paint an optimistic picture of a city on the verge of significant transformation. Through innovative urban planning strategies and sustainable development strategies, Halifax will soon become an exemplar of sustainable development and innovation. Residents eagerly anticipate Halifax's infrastructure renaissance - an unfolding that promises to reshape its cityscape while enriching lives across generations - that promises to reshape cityscape and enrich community lives for decades ahead. Plans have already begun and we look forward to an brighter, greener and more connected Halifax.

# Regional Folklore and Myths

Fairies and Fae Folk: The Enchanters of the Forests In the depths of the ancient woodlands, where the leaves whisper and the wind sings, dwell the elusive beings of myth and legend — fairies and fae folk. As old as the forests themselves and as varied as the creatures that inhabit the earth, these mystical entities continue to captivate us with their charm and their close association with nature's unspoiled beauty. This article delves into the ethereal world of the fairies and how they symbolize the urgent need for environmental preservation. The Origins of Fairy Lore The belief in fairies is rooted deeply in the folk traditions around the globe, from the Celtic lands with their Sidhe to Scandinavian tales of the Huldra. Renowned folklorist Dr. Elaine O'Sullivan, who has dedicated her life to studying the fae, suggests that these legends likely arose as interpretations of natural phenomena and as a way to explain the inexplicable. By September 2023, her extensive research has further emphasized that beyond mere storytelling, they serve a greater purpose: reminding us of our intrinsic connection to the natural world. Dr. O'Sullivan's texts, such as "Whispers of the Green: Understanding Fae Folklore" (published on September 15, 2023), investigates the possibility that these tales were early attempts to personify the forces of nature. The Many Faces of the Fae The fae folk take on many forms, reflecting the diversity of nature itself. Some are diminutive winged beings, emblems of the delicacy and intricacy of ecosystems, while others are grand and noble creatures that seem to command the very roots of the earth. Not all are benign, and stories about mischievous sprites or malevolent goblins serve as reminders that nature is not just a provider but also a powerful, sometimes indifferent force. On November 5, 2023, at the Annual Global Folklore Symposium, fiction writer Michaela Thompson shared tales she grew up with of the 'Good People', cautioning against disrespecting nature for fear of their wrath. The Fae and Environmentalism: A Modern Bond In a contemporary context, the imagery and symbolism of the fae are potent tools for raising environmental awareness. Their tales, a cautionary note about the perils of overstepping our bounds with nature, have found resonance among modern environmental movements. A report named "Myth in Green: Fairies as Advocates for Conservation" by ecologist Dr. Robert Hale on October 31, 2023, drew parallels between stories of woodland fairies and the real endangered species that inhabit our old-growth forests. He argued that the mystique surrounding fairies can encourage the preservation of the very habitats that inspire their lore. The Fae as Guardians of the Green When delving into fairy folklore, one theme consistently emerges: fairies as protectors of the wild. They are often depicted with a fierce territorial attachment to their woodland realms, acting as stewards of the flora and fauna therein. This is particularly striking in cultures where reverence for nature spirits is integrated into ecological management. For instance, historian Anna-Maria Mikhailova has noted on several occasions, most recently in her book "The Green Mantle: Fae Folk in Slavic Tradition" (released on September 22, 2023), that the Eastern European belief in protective forest spirits can still influence contemporary attitudes towards conservation. Reconnecting with Nature Through Fairy Tales One might wonder, in the digital age, how the ancient tales of fairies fit into our modern lives. Through storytelling, people can foster a deeper connection with nature, especially during an era where technological detachment is prevalent. Sociologist Liam Peterson's paper, "In the Shadow of the Fae: Modern Disconnection and Ancient Tales," published on October 10, 2023, explored how the resurgence of interest in fairies could help bridge the gap between humanity and the natural world. By embracing these stories, communities may find renewed interest in the preservation and celebration of local ecosystems. The Woods as Haven for the Hidden Folk Central to fairy tales is the motif of the forest as a liminal space, a border between worlds where anything is possible. It is also a vital habitat, pulsating with life and under threat from deforestation and climate change. The enchanting idea that fairies dwell in these places can be a powerful force for mobilizing efforts to protect them. Activist groups have been known to invoke images of fairies in campaigns aimed at halting logging or development in ancient woodlands, such as in the case of the 'Save the Green Haven' initiative back in May 2024. The Call to Action: Heeding the Fae's Message The message implicit in fairy folklore is clear: the natural world is precious, mysterious, and worth defending. It helps us remember that we are not separate from the ecosystems we inhabit but rather a part of a broader, interwoven tapestry of life. Environmentalists, like the young and vibrant Marissa Quezada who founded the 'Fae's Whisper' movement in June 2023, have embraced these myths not only as stories but as mandates for action, embodying the message of harmony and protection that fairies have always stood for. In closing, the fairies and fae folk, enchanting and elusive, are far more than mere figments of antiquated beliefs or simplistic stories to delight children. They serve as gentle yet urgent reminders of our environmental responsibilities. In our pursuit of progress, we must look to the wisdom of the enchanters of the forests to guide us toward a more respectful and sustainable relationship with the world around us. Whether we see them as literal beings or symbolic harbingers, their legacy persists in the whisper of the leaves, the murmur of the streams, and in every corner of the emerald enclaves they call home.

# Supplemental Security Income (SSI) Benefits | Social Security Administration#2

The Supplemental Security Income SSI program pays benefits to disabled adults and children who have limited income and resources. SSI benefits also are payable to people 65 and older without disabilities who meet the financial limits. People who have worked long enough may also be able to receive Social Security disability or retirement benefits as well as SSI. Learn more about SSI How Do I Apply For SSI? If you would like to apply for SSI benefits for : An adult with a disability You can now file for Supplemental Security Income SSI online but only if you meet certain requirements. You are eligible to file online for SSI if you : Are between the ages of 18 and 65 ; Have never been married ; Aren t blind ; Are a U.S. citizen residing in one of the fifty states, District of Columbia, or the Northern Mariana Islands ; Haven t applied for or received SSI benefits in the past ; and Are applying for Social Security Disability Insurance at the same time as your SSI claim. Find out if you are eligible to receive Social Security Disability Benefits. Apply Online for SSI and Disability Benefits Once you finish the online process , a Social Security representative will contact you for any additional information needed for the applications. You can also schedule an appointment with a local Social Security office to file an application. Call 1 - 800 - 772 - 1213 TTY 1 - 800 - 325 - 0778 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. , Monday through Friday or contact your local Social Security office. Learn more and start the disability process at our Disability Benefits page. A child with a disability Social Security does not yet have an online application for Children s benefits. Visit Apply For Disability Benefits Child Under Age 18 to apply for your child. You can complete the online Child Disability Report if you are ready to apply now. A person age 65 and older Call us at 1 - 800 - 772 - 1213. If you are deaf or hard of hearing , you can call us at TTY 1 - 800 - 325 - 0778 , or Visit your local Social Security office. Call first to make an appointment. Can I File An Appeal If My SSI Application Was Denied? Yes , if we recently denied your SSI application , you can file an SSI appeal online. # Local Technology and Innovation

Virtual Reality and Augmented Reality: Pioneering the Future of Education In recent years, we have seen a dramatic shift in educational methodologies, triggered by swift advancements in technology. Notably catching the eye are Virtual Reality (VR) and Augmented Reality (AR), which are increasingly being integrated into educational settings. To unravel the impact and potential of VR and AR in education, Local Technology and Innovation engaged in a series of interviews with experts who are at the forefront of this educational revolution. The Emergence of Immersive Learning It was on a crisp autumn morning on September 15, 2023, when we met Dr. Emily Stanton, a professor of Educational Technology at New Horizons University, who has been integrating VR in her classroom for the past two years. Dr. Stanton explained the benefits of immersive learning, "VR takes students to places they couldn't otherwise go. Picture a biology class where students can travel inside the human body or a history lesson where they can witness historical events unfold." Moving beyond the confines of traditional classrooms, VR and AR offer immersive experiences that enhance understanding and retention. They cater to various learning styles and allow for experiential learning that can be both captivating and educational. Transforming Theory into Practice Johnathan Reeves, a tech entrepreneur and the founder of EduTech Innovations, shared his insights on September 29, 2023, about how VR and AR are more than just gadgets—they're tools that can revolutionize education when applied wisely. "When students use VR and AR, they're not just passive recipients of information. They interact with the content. This active engagement is a game-changer," stated Reeves. The hands-on aspect of VR and AR can bridge the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical application. For example, in vocational training, students can practice complex procedures in a simulated environment before tackling real-world scenarios. Challenges and Solutions in Implementation Yet, integrating VR and AR in education is not free from challenges. During a round-table discussion on October 8, 2023, various stakeholders, including educators, technologists, and policymakers, addressed issues such as accessibility, cost, and the learning curve associated with these technologies. Software developer and AR specialist, Anita Singh, highlighted the importance of designing user-friendly interfaces to ensure educational AR apps are accessible to all ages and abilities. "Creating intuitive AR platforms allows for seamless integration into the classroom, reducing the intimidation factor that often comes with new technologies," remarked Singh. While cost remains a significant concern, especially for underfunded educational institutions, increased adoption and technological advances have started to drive prices down. There's optimism that VR and AR will become more accessible as more educators recognize their value. The Human Element in Virtual Worlds Surprisingly, one of the most poignant moments of our interviews came on November 12, 2023, when we spoke to Michael Torres, an educational psychologist specializing in digital learning environments. Torres stressed the importance of the human element in virtual spaces. "VR and AR should not replace educators but rather serve to accentuate their teaching," he said. The sentiment echoed across our conversations, emphasizing the role of VR and AR as supplementary tools. According to Torres, educators are irreplaceable guides who provide context, facilitate discussion, and foster an environment of curiosity. VR and AR are tools that, when paired with a teacher's direction, can elevate the learning experience. Case Studies: Successful VR and AR Applications On-the-ground examples of VR and AR's impact in the classroom were especially illuminating. During November 20, 2023, we spoke with Sarah Zheng, a science teacher from Springfield High, who shared her success story using VR headsets to teach astronomy. "Students explored the universe in a way a textbook could never convey. Their excitement was palpable, and their grasp of concepts significantly improved," Zheng commented. At Riverdale Vocational College, instructor Mark Davidson adopted AR for automotive engineering courses. "Using AR headsets, students can see the intricacies of a car's engine overlaid on a physical vehicle, allowing them to understand and retain complex information readily," Davidson shared on December 1, 2023. Beyond The Classroom: Future Prospects Looking beyond the classroom, VR and AR hold potential for lifelong learning and professional development. On December 15, 2023, corporate trainer Lisa McQuillan shared her thoughts on this. "These technologies are highly adaptable for corporate training, healthcare, and beyond. We're only scratching the surface of their application," she opined. Indeed, VR and AR have been deployed in diverse fields, from medical training simulations that help surgeons prepare for operations, to virtual tours that enable real estate agents to show properties without the client ever stepping foot inside. The Shift in Educational Paradigms The experts agree: VR and AR are more than just novel educational tools—they signify a paradigm shift in how we approach teaching and learning. As we continue to explore and harness their capabilities, these technologies have the potential to make education more engaging, accessible, and effective. The conversations with these pioneers in the VR/AR educational space underscore a collective vision of a future where learning is an immersive and interactive experience, geared towards fostering a deeper understanding and enthusiasm for knowledge in students of all ages. With continuous development and dialogue between technologists, educators, and policymakers, VR and AR will likely become staples in educational institutions worldwide. As they evolve and improve, the focus will remain on how these tools can enrich the educational experience without overshadowing the fundamental human connections that are at the heart of learning.
