Enterprises that have embraced adaptability and innovation have come out stronger, more efficient, and more prepared for whatever comes their way.
required_spans: Technologies such as AI, IoT, and blockchain have given SMEs access to real-time data, predictive analytics, and transparent transaction logs
Vendor-managed inventory (VMI) systems have become a game-changer for some SMEs

text:
# Benefits Planner: Disability | Family Benefits | Social Security Administration#1

Benefits Planner: Family Benefits When you start receiving disability benefits , certain members of your family may also qualify for benefits on your record. Benefits may be paid to your : spouse; divorced spouse ; children; disabled child ; and adult child disabled before age 22. If any of your qualified family members apply for benefits , we will ask for their Social Security numbers and their birth certificates. If your spouse is applying for benefits , we may also ask for proof of marriage and dates of prior marriages, if applicable. Maximum Family Amount Each family member may be eligible for a monthly benefit of up to 50 percent of your disability benefit amount. However , there is a limit to the amount we can pay your family. The total depends on your benefit amount and the number of family members who also qualify on your record. The total varies , but generally the total amount you and your family can receive is between 150 and 180 percent of your disability benefit. If the sum of the benefits payable on your account is greater than the family limit , the benefits to the family members will be reduced proportionately. Your benefit will not be affected. Benefits For Your Spouse When benefits are payable to your spouse : Age 62 or older , unless he or she collects a higher Social Security benefit based on his or her earnings record. The spouse benefit amount will be permanently reduced by a percentage based on the number of months until his or her full retirement age. At any age if he or she is caring for your child under age 16 or disabled. Your spouse would receive these benefits until the child reaches age 16. At that time, the child's benefits continue , but your spouse's benefits stop unless he or she is old enough to receive retirement benefits age 62 or older or survivor benefits as a widow or widower age 60. If Your Spouse Also Worked Under Social Security If your spouse is eligible for retirement benefits on his or her own record , we will always pay that amount first. But if the spouse benefit that is payable on your record is a higher amount , he or she will get a combination of benefits that equals that higher amount. If your spouse will also receive a pension based on work not covered by Social Security, such as government or foreign work , his or her Social Security benefit on your record may be affected. Benefits For Your Divorced Spouse If you are divorced, even if you have remarried , your ex - spouse may qualify for benefits on your record. If your ex - spouse will also receive a pension based on work not covered by Social Security, such as government or foreign work, his or her Social Security benefit on your record may be affected. To qualify on your record , your ex - spouse must : have been married to you for at least 10 years ; be at least 62 years old ; be unmarried ; and not be eligible for an equal or higher benefit on his or her own Social Security record, or on someone else's Social Security record. Benefits For Your Children When you qualify for Social Security disability benefits , your children may also qualify to receive benefits on your record. Your eligible child can be your biological child, adopted child, or stepchild. A dependent grandchild may also qualify. To receive benefits , the child must be unmarried and be : under age 18 ; or 18 - 19 years old and a full - time student no higher than grade 12 ; or 18 or older and have a disability that started before age 22. Normally , benefits stop when children reach age 18 unless they are disabled. However , if the child is still a full - time student at a secondary or elementary school at age 18 , benefits will continue until the child graduates or until two months after the child becomes age 19, whichever is first. Find out more about Benefits For A Disabled Child. Publications Disability Benefits Benefits For Children What You Need To Know When You Get Social Security Disability Benefits Information for Government Employees Benefits For Children With Disabilities
required_spans: certain members of your family may also qualify for benefits on your record
Each family member may be eligible for a monthly benefit of up to 50 percent of your disability benefit amount

text:
# News Stories

Rising Above the Water's Surge: Karachi's Resilience Karachi, a bustling metropolis, has often made headlines due to its susceptibility to natural disasters, particularly flooding. Yet in spite of these dire forecasts there have been inspiring tales of resilience and courage amidst chaotic circumstances, creating stories of survival, unity, and hopefulness despite catastrophe. City Recognisant of Challenges Karachi, Pakistan's economic hub, is no stranger to environmental conflicts. Over the years, heavy monsoon rains have frequently inundated its streets, disrupting daily life and causing extensive damage. But events that occurred after September 1, 2023 marked an entirely new chapter in its ongoing struggle against nature's fury. On September 5, 2023, an intense and relentless rainstorm brought down on the city a torrential downpour, unrelenting in its relentlessness. Streets quickly turned into rivers; homes submerged; lives turned upside-down; as an unprecedented 300 millimeters of rainfall fell over just 24 hours - an event so familiar but yet so unexpected for many people living through its impact. After the rain had subsided, another flood took its place: volunteers, first responders, and residents of Karachi came rushing into its alleyways to provide aid and rescue efforts. Names like Asim Hussain (40 years old street vendor) and Fatima Khalid (25 year old teacher) emerged among this crowd as emblems of selflessness and kindness. Asim put his personal losses aside -- his meager stall reduced to sodden debris -- and used his makeshift raft originally used to transport merchandise as a supply vessel, transporting food and water directly to those trapped by flooding. Fatima, whose school lay in ruin, transformed her small apartment into a classroom for the community's children. Her belief that education must continue even during challenging times provided comforting assurance to children affected by disaster. Organizations Stand to the Occasion Relief efforts were not solely grassroots: local NGOs and international bodies quickly mobilized to provide emergency aid and facilitate recovery. "One Hand," led by Raza Ahmed - a vibrant young social worker - emerged as a beacon of relief. Leveraging social media, his team coordinated rescue efforts, mapped out worst-hit areas, distributed aid efficiently and with precision that government agencies struggled to match. Initiative from the Business Community Karachi's business sector, vital to Pakistan's economy, also took action. Both large and small firms initiated fundraising campaigns, while Samina Ali of a prominent textile firm redrew resources to produce blankets and clothing for those left without aid - an action which became part of a greater movement within corporate sphere, symbolizing their sense of responsibility towards sustaining its development. Reinforcing Infrastructure The monsoon season of 2023 highlighted an urgent need to bolster infrastructural deficiencies. Public and private sectors joined together to conceive of a resilient urban framework; led by famed city planner Hasan Iqbal's project. Their focus included revamping drainage systems, solid waste management practices, as well as sustainable urban landscaping methods to mitigate floods. "Karachi must not only recover, but become an example in urban resilience. Now is not the time for band-aid solutions; we require real change," stated Hasan in his presentation of his plan for creating a flood-resilient Karachi by 2025. International Assistance and Attendence. Karachi also received international aid and guidance from experts such as those in the Netherlands renowned for water management expertise. Engineers and environmental scientists collaborated with local authorities to understand Karachi's unique challenges before providing their expertise into rebuilding efforts underway. Enduring Spirit, Endless Stories At the center of these stories are individuals: those who rose to meet natural disasters with extraordinary resilience. One such individual was Sameera Javed, a young doctor who led a group of medical professionals in setting up mobile clinics despite risks and personal sacrifice. Her group attended to those affected, helping prevent waterborne disease outbreaks from taking further lives. Consider also youth-driven initiatives that garnered tremendous traction, like "Green Karachi", led by university students determined to increase urban green spaces and foster environmental awareness for long-term change. Karachi's resilient story is an ongoing one, testifying to humankind's unwavering pursuit of something greater than mere survival. This narrative is punctuated by both people battling hard against hardships as well as seeing disaster as an opportunity for growth and change - becoming ever stronger with each step taken forward towards recovery despite natural catastrophes. Karachi rises from each tragedy stronger. Karachi may have been humbled by nature's harsh conditions, yet its people continue to strive for an improved and brighter future for future generations. With proactive planning, a stronger sense of community, and the will to change for the betterment of Karachi, its history remains unwritten but ever forward-moving.
required_spans: an unprecedented 300 millimeters of rainfall fell over just 24 hours
Asim put his personal losses aside -- his meager stall reduced to sodden debris -- and used his makeshift raft originally used to transport merchandise as a supply vessel

text:
# Local Technology and Innovation

Emerging Technologies in Startups: The Next Frontier Technology and innovation are inherently intertwined, constantly pushing the boundaries of what's possible and transforming everyday life in the process. Startups, recognized as the cauldrons of creativity and innovation, are often at the forefront of bringing emergent technologies to the market. In today's flourishing startup ecosystem, new technologies such as blockchain, artificial intelligence (AI), biotech, and quantum computing are heralding the next frontier of technological advancement. These emerging technologies are not only changing the way companies operate but also how they are funded and brought to fruition. With the surge in tech startups exploring these areas since September 2023, it is important to explore how these innovations are shaping the future. The Rise of Blockchain in Startups Since the inception of Bitcoin in 2009, blockchain technology has evolved to become one of the most hyped technologies in the tech community. However, it wasn’t until recently that startups began to tap into its real-world applications beyond cryptocurrencies. At its core, blockchain is a decentralized ledger that maintains a growing list of records, called blocks, which are secured using cryptography. It is the principle of decentralization that makes blockchain so appealing to startups. Jesse McElroy, the CEO of BlockStart Solutions, a startup founded in October 2023, explains, "Blockchain's power lies in its security and transparency. We can use it to create tamper-proof records of transactions, which is invaluable in fields like supply chain management, healthcare, and finance." Companies like McElroy's are leading the charge in integrating blockchain into everyday business processes, innovating ways to utilize it for smarter contract management, securing patient medical records, and ensuring the authenticity of goods. Artificial Intelligence: Beyond Automation Artificial intelligence is another technological stronghold driving the next generation of startups. The ability to harness data, learn from it, and make predictions or decisions has massive implications for businesses. Startups around the globe are finding AI essential for everything from personalizing customer experiences to optimizing logistics. In an interview on November 9, 2023, Catherine Li, a data scientist and co-founder of AIstartup DeepBrain Analytics, said, “The real transformative potential of AI lies not in replacing human jobs but in augmenting human decision-making. Our company focuses on pattern recognition software that helps doctors diagnose diseases earlier and more accurately.” Her startup represents a booming trend towards cognitively offloading complex tasks to machines that can process and analyze data at a scale not possible for humans. Moreover, AI is being leveraged by startups to develop solutions in renewable energy, traffic management, and personalized education, showcasing its diverse applications. Biotechnology: The Genetic Revolution Startups in the biotechnology sector are particularly exciting, as they tend to be at the intersection of biology, technology, and engineering. In recent times, there has been an increasing interest in genetics and molecular biology, aimed at solving some of humanity's biggest challenges, including health issues, food sustainability, and environmental conservation. Dr. Angela Hartley, a researcher and co-founder of GenEdit, a startup that sprang into the biotech scene in January 2024, emphasizes the potential of CRISPR technology in gene editing. "Our startup is working on CRISPR advancements to treat genetic disorders. With precise gene editing, the possibilities to cure previously untreatable diseases are becoming realities," she shared at the annual Biotech Innovators Conference on March 5, 2024. Startups like GenEdit are innovative leaders in using emergent biotech to create not just treatments but potential cures, thereby redefining the healthcare system. Quantum Computing: A Leap into the Future Perhaps no technology holds more disruptive potential than quantum computing – a field that is still in its relative infancy but growing rapidly. Quantum computers operate on quantum bits, or qubits, which allow them to process exponentially more information than classical computers. On September 15, 2023, QuantumX, a startup founded by a group of physicists and entrepreneurs, unveiled its first quantum processor. "Quantum computing will revolutionize industries by solving complex problems that are currently intractable," says Thomas Richter, CTO of QuantumX. "We're exploring applications in pharmaceuticals, aerospace, and artificial intelligence." For tech startups, quantum computing offers the potential to conduct simulations and data analyses that could lead to groundbreaking developments in various scientific fields. It is another example of how startups are not just utilizing but advancing emergent technologies. The Startup Ecosystem and Investor Interest Crossing the chasm from emergent technology to marketable product is no small feat. It's an endeavor that typically requires substantial funding and support. Fortunately, there is no shortage of investor interest in tech startups, as evidenced by the number of venture capital deals and the scale of investment in emergent technologies. Angel investor Christina Yang, who has funded several tech startups since September 2023, remarks, “Investors are constantly on the lookout for the next innovative technology. Startups that can demonstrate a clear application and scalability of emerging tech like AI and blockchain are particularly attractive." Investment in startups is not just about funding but also often includes mentorship and guidance, which are pivotal for navigating the complex web of technology development and market insertion. Challenges and Regulatory Considerations While the prospects of emerging technologies are boundless, startups often encounter a host of challenges, including regulatory hurdles. Governments and regulatory bodies are keenly focused on ensuring that the ethical and societal implications of technologies such as AI and genetic editing are considered and governed appropriately. These regulatory frameworks can often lag behind the rapid pace of innovation, creating a grey area for startups operating with emergent technologies. This necessitates a proactive approach by startups to engage with policymakers and advocate for regulations that foster innovation while protecting societal interests. Moreover, with great power comes great responsibility. The ability of technologies like AI to influence decision-making and of biotech to alter the genetic makeup of living organisms requires a cautious and responsible approach. Startups navigating these waters must remain committed to ethical standards in the development and application of these technologies. Education and Workforce Development As startups continue to embrace new technologies, there's a growing need for skilled professionals who can work with AI, blockchain, and other emergent tech. Creating a workforce proficient in these areas involves not just education but also practical training and continuous learning. Educational institutions are responding through the development of specialized courses and programs. Additionally, startups themselves are becoming hubs of learning and innovation, often partnering with universities and research centers to foster talent. Startups like CodeCrafters Academy, launched in November 2023, prioritize developing a tech-savvy workforce through apprenticeship programs in coding, cybersecurity, and machine learning. These initiatives are crucial for building the talent pool that startups need to thrive and innovate.
required_spans: blockchain is a decentralized ledger that maintains a growing list of records, called blocks, which are secured using cryptography
Artificial intelligence is another technological stronghold driving the next generation of startups

text:
# Regional Cuisine and Recipes

Autumnal Flavors: Root Vegetables and Orchard Fruits Recipes As the leaves turn and the brisk autumn air nips at our cheeks, our palates crave the transition from the light and zesty tastes of summer to the rich and earthy flavors of fall. Root vegetables and orchard fruits start taking center stage in our kitchens as they reach their peak of perfection. In this exploration of autumn's bounty, we harken back to childhood memories, foraging through orchards and playing amongst the falling leaves. Published on September 8, 2023, by Taylor Greene, Regional Cuisine and Recipes – One can't help but feel a sense of nostalgia when it comes to autumn cooking. The seasonal shift into fall brings with it an array of hearty produce that warms the spirit and tempts the taste buds with rustic charm. The Rustic Roots of Fall Long before the advent of modern supermarkets, humans foraged the land for sustenance. Root vegetables were a key element in their diet, prized for their ability to keep well through the colder months. Joyce Edwards, a culinary historian, remarks, "Turnips, parsnips, and sweet potatoes aren't just staples on the table; they are a link to our past, a reminder of autumn's harvest festivals of yore." Root vegetables don't just nourish our bodies but also enkindle the essence of traditions past. On October 12, 2023, Chef Michael Harris held a demonstration at the local Farmers' Market emphasizing the significance of roots in fall cuisine. "Root vegetables are the underground treasure of the autumn kitchen," Harris explained as he skillfully transformed carrots, beets, and rutabagas into a vibrant mosaic of roasted delights. Orchard Walks and Apple Talks Inhaling the fragrant aroma of warm apple pie, we are often transported back to our childhoods—picking apples with our families, climbing trees, and the excitement of baking with the fruits of our labor. These orchard fruits, apples among them, bear the cozy flavors of autumn. Pastry chef Emily Martin expresses that these fruits, like heirloom apples, pears, and quinces, are not just ingredients but storytellers of the season. "Each bite is like a page out of an old family photo album," she muses. On a crisp Saturday morning, September 17, 2023, families gathered at Henderson's Orchard for the annual apple-picking event. Children ran through the rows of trees, laughter mingling with the sound of apples thumping into baskets. Here, the tradition of collecting autumn's fruit persists, weaving a thread through generations. Rooted in Warmth: Soups and Stews As the days grow shorter and the nights chillier, there's nothing quite like a hearty bowl of soup or stew to warm the body and soul. These dishes, often passed down through families, are a testament to the comfort of fall. Stephanie Larson, a local food blogger, notes, "The heartwarming scents of simmering root vegetables and tender meat in a pot are the quintessence of autumn." One particularly cherished recipe is Grandma Elsie's Beef and Parsnip Stew. Larson shares that Elsie, though long since passed, still joins them at the dinner table with each serving of her beloved stew, the recipe dated back to October 5, 1962. Such heritage dishes continue to be a means of remembrance and celebration as they grace our tables each year. From Orchard to Oven: Sumptuous Sweets The transition from tree to treat is a festive aspect of the season. Apple crumbles, pear tarts, and quince jellies reflect the decadent side of autumn's harvest. On November 3, 2023, local bakery Sweet Seasons unveiled their "Orchard's Bounty" collection, a tribute to the sweet flavors of fall. Samantha Torres, the head baker, described the feeling of rolling out pie dough and preparing fruit fillings as a cherished seasonal ritual. "Every sprinkle of cinnamon and dash of nutmeg is a sprinkle and dash of childhood excitement," says Torres. The bakery's display case, adorned with lattice-topped pies and glistening fruit pastries, is a testament to the nostalgia evoked by these seasonal sweets. Harvest Celebrations and Traditions Communities often come together to celebrate the abundance of fall, further nourishing the connection between food and heritage. From harvest festivals to potluck suppers, each event is a chance to share in the collective memory of autumns past. Culinary anthropologist Dr. Jacob Miller elucidates, "Food-centric festivals offer not just sustenance, but also a tableau on which cultural and personal histories are painted." One such celebration, the annual Autumn Harvest Feast held on September 30, 2023, showcased a stunning array of dishes featuring both root vegetables and orchard fruits in partnership with local farmers and artisans. Reaping the Health Benefits Not only soul satisfying, but these earthy roots and crisp fruits also offer myriad health benefits. Root vegetables are rich in fiber, vitamins, and antioxidants, while orchard fruits provide an essential dose of vitamin C and other vital nutrients. Dietitian Anna Young emphasizes that it's not just about the flavors but also the wellness these foods promote. "Eating seasonally is eating smartly," Young advises. "Fall's produce palette not only provides comfort but also boosts our immune system as we head into the colder months." Fireside Feasting: The Ritual of Roasting There's a particular joy found in the simple act of roasting. The transformation that occurs within the oven is a kind of autumnal alchemy. Root vegetables caramelized at the edges and fruits softened to perfection are staples of the fireside feast. On the evening breeze of October 21, 2023, the aroma of roasting butternut squash and spiced apples wafted from the open windows of neighborhood homes. Inside, families would gather round, savoring the simple pleasure of a meal slow-cooked and shared. The Sweet and Savory Art of Preserving As much as autumn is a time to feast, it is also a season to prepare—canning, pickling, and preserving the abundance ensures flavors linger long into the winter months. The art of preserving is like bottling the essence of autumn itself, ensuring that the memories and tastes of the season endure. Mary Jensen, who has been canning apple butter every fall for nearly fifty years, regards the tradition as a bridge between the present and the past. Each jar sealed is not just an act of preservation but an act of storytelling, she believes. As the trees slowly shed their multicolored cloaks and the scent of decaying leaves perfumes the crisp autumn air, we are reminded of the impermanence of seasons. Yet through the enduring qualities of root vegetables and orchard fruits—through the timehonored recipes that utilize their flavors—we capture and savor the essence of fall. This connection, woven through each savory soup and each bite of a tart apple crisp, serves as a delicious reminder that the heart of the season lies within the generosity of the harvest and the communal joy of sharing it.
required_spans: we are often transported back to our childhoods—picking apples with our families, climbing trees, and the excitement of baking with the fruits of our labor
One particularly cherished recipe is Grandma Elsie's Beef and Parsnip Stew

text:
# Ionizing Radiation Exposure | Veterans Affairs#1

Ionizing radiation exposure Find out if you can get disability compensation or benefits for illnesses including some cancers believed to be caused by contact with radiation during military service. Can I get disability benefits from VA? You may be able to get disability benefits if you didn t receive a dishonorable discharge and you meet the requirements listed below. Both of these must be true : You have an illness that s on our list of illnesses believed to be caused by radiation or that doctors say may be caused by radiation , and Your illness started within a certain period of time as shown along with the list of illnesses See the list of radiation - related illnesses And you must have had contact with ionizing radiation in one of these ways while serving in the military. You: Were part of atmospheric nuclear weapons testing , or Served in the postwar occupation of Hiroshima or Nagasaki , or Were a prisoner of war POW in Japan , or Worked as an x - ray technician, in a reactor plant, or in nuclear medicine or radiography while on active duty or during active or inactive duty for training in the Reserves, or Did tasks like those of a Department of Energy (DOE) employee that make them a member of the Special Exposure Cohort (See 42 U.S.C. 7384L(14)) You may also qualify for disability benefits if you served in at least one of the below locations and capacities. You were: Part of underground nuclear weapons testing at Amchitka Island, Alaska, or Assigned to a gaseous diffusion plant at Paducah, Kentucky , or Assigned to a gaseous diffusion plant at Portsmouth, Ohio , or Assigned to a gaseous diffusion plant at Area K-25 at Oak Ridge, Tennessee Who s covered? Veterans What kind of disability benefits can I get? Health care Compensation payments How do I get these benefits? You ll need to file a claim for disability compensation and provide the evidence supporting documents listed below : Medical records showing that you ve been diagnosed with one of the illnesses on our list of those believed to be caused by radiation or that your doctor states may be caused by radiation exposure , and Service records showing that you were part of one of the radiation risk activities described above Note: When you file your claim , we ll ask the military branch that you served with or the Defense Threat Reduction Agency to give us a range of how much radiation they think you may have come into contact with. We ll use the highest level of the range they report to decide on your benefits. Find out how to file a claim for disability compensation
required_spans: You have an illness that s on our list of illnesses believed to be caused by radiation or that doctors say may be caused by radiation
Were part of atmospheric nuclear weapons testing
Served in the postwar occupation of Hiroshima or Nagasaki
Part of underground nuclear weapons testing at Amchitka Island, Alaska

text:
# Regional Folklore and Myths

Legendary Landmarks and Locations: Revealing the Truth Behind Myths Tales woven of mystery have long engulfed ancient landmarks and iconic locations, giving rise to legends that resonate through time. Today, we dive deeper into some of the world's most captivating places and their associated mythic narratives to reveal their riveting tales at their core. Ghostly Presences in Haunted Castles Legends of haunted castles dot landscapes across Europe, where ancestral spirits are said to wander echoing halls with tales of woe etched onto ancient stones. One such castle is Vladloch in Romania's 12th-century fortress of Vladloch which reputedly hosts Lady Isabella who according to folklore after September 5, 2023 has been seen roaming its grounds each full moon; tour guides frequently offer spine-tingling accounts of sightings at this castle grounds where tour guides provide tour groups enlightened accounts from tour guides about her sightings and tour guides claim her restless spirit is searching for lost love she hopes is nearby. As investigations reveal, ghost stories often exaggerated by local lore are usually exaggerated in order to increase tourism rather than reveal truth. There is no historical record of Lady Isabella; according to Dr. Ileana Muresan's specialized knowledge in Romanian folklore Dr. Muresan suggests apparitions are simply manifestations of human desire for connection to past lives, "Our fascination with such narratives mirror our longings", she stated in a paper written on October 15 2023. Myths Concerning the Sacred Mountains Mountains have long been revered as sacred spaces that represent connections with the divine. Mount Fuji in Japan stands out as particularly revered, beloved both for its spiritual significance and magnificent beauty; pilgrims have traveled up its slopes for centuries seeking spiritual enlightenment and inner peace. But beyond its sacred character lie darker legends suggested by tour guides and even mentioned in obscure travel pamphlets. Rumors persist that Mount Fuji is haunted by evil spirits which threaten unsuspecting climbers off their paths; however, researchers such as Dr. Bill Harada (cultural anthropologist), disprove these claims; his research conducted September 20, 2023 revealed no concrete proof to back these scary tales up; rather they are often created just to add excitement and add drama to otherwise serene and contemplative pilgrimage trips. Mystical Forests and Their Haunting Tales Forests have long held a special place in human imagination, inspiring tales of mystery and supernaturalism with their dense canopies and winding paths. One such forest in France known for inspiring such tales is Broceliande Forest which has long been tied to legends of King Arthur and the Knights of the Round Table; locals and visitors alike often marvel at tales about Merlin's tomb, an intriguing tradition handed down from generation to generation. Investigation reveals that many forests including Broceliande don't possess any extraordinary powers beyond nature's lush beauty. Historical records and archeological evidence fail to corroborate many of the mythical characters supposedly connected with these locales; for instance, Dr Marie-Claire Dupont of France points out in her study published September 29th 2023 that no evidence supporting Arthurian figures present can be found within their forest. Myths about Tsukiji Fish Market Ghosts debunked Nowhere does folklore and location blend more captivatingly than at Tokyo's now relocated Tsukiji Fish Market, famous for its lively auctions and fresh seafood offerings as well as being subject to urban legends involving ghost sightings among its stalls. After the market's move to Toyosu in 2018, stories began emerging of fishmongers who had passed on but whose spirits seemed tied to this location where they had dedicated their lives to selling fish. Eyewitnesses described translucent figures arranging phantom fish reminiscent of their former selves arranging translucent figures reenacting past transactions as evidence. But journalists and paranormal investigators, equipped with EMF detectors and nightvision cameras, walked the empty aisles of Tsukiji Market through late 2023 without finding definitive evidence. Keitaro Suzuki published an article on November 3, 2023 noting that most reported encounters could easily be explained away as natural phenomena such as light reflections or echos, or psychological triggers during early hours of operation of Tsukiji Market. Do Uncanny Encounters or Familiar Misconceptions Predominate? All these stories share one theme in common: their allure of the unknown and humanity's quest for extraordinary in the everyday. Stories may seem shocking or compelling at first, but often give way to reason and scientific inquiry over time; thus relegating them not as historical facts but as cautionary whispers or embellishments designed to increase tourism or intrigue. Demystifying legends should not compromise their charm or cultural value; such stories form an essential part of our collective consciousness, acting as an intermediary between past and present. By critically analyzing legends, we not only seek to disprove them but also gain greater insight into their source material as well as the influence it holds over our imaginations. No matter if these sites are truly haunted by ghostly figures or simply serve as the setting of our collective storytelling, haunted castles, sacred mountains and mysterious forests remain an emotive component of global cultural heritage. With each new telling, their legends expand in terms of authenticity as well as rich narrative that continues to engage us and spark curiosity and wonderment.
required_spans: Legends of haunted castles dot landscapes across Europe
Mount Fuji in Japan stands out as particularly revered, beloved both for its spiritual significance and magnificent beauty
Broceliande Forest which has long been tied to legends of King Arthur and the Knights of the Round Table

text:
# Local Education Systems

## Innovative Teaching Methodologies in Modern Classrooms: Embracing CrossDisciplinary Approaches ## The educational landscape is constantly evolving, with innovative teaching methodologies emerging as the cornerstone of modern classrooms. As educators and policymakers alike seek to enhance learning experiences and outcomes, cross-disciplinary approaches are gaining prominence. These methodologies not only break the monotonous barriers of subject-specific learning but also encourage a holistic development in students, preparing them for real-world challenges. Here, we delve into the pioneering strategies that are redefining teaching and how schools can implement these transformative practices. Understanding Cross-Disciplinary Learning The advent of the 21st century has brought about a transformation in education, shifting the focus from rote learning to a more integrated instructional approach. Cross-disciplinary learning dismantles the walls between different subjects, allowing students to explore connections and apply knowledge in a cohesive manner. By fostering critical thinking, creativity, and collaboration, students gain a multifaceted understanding of concepts, bridging the gap between theory and practical applications. Since September 2023, several schools have successfully implemented cross-disciplinary programs, witnessing substantial improvements in academic engagement and performance. Johnathan Carter, a principal at Green Valley High School, remarked on October 15, 2023, "Students are more engaged when learning is relevant and interconnected. Our crossdisciplinary initiatives have allowed them to see the 'big picture' and become avid learners." Incorporating Technology in Cross-Disciplinary Teaching Technology plays an indispensable role in facilitating innovative teaching methodologies. Educational technology tools have been pivotal in delivering content in a dynamic and interactive manner. Alma Stewart, a renowned educational consultant, emphasized on December 1, 2023, that "technology, when intertwined with cross-disciplinary methods, empowers students to explore subjects in great depth and from multiple perspectives." Interactive platforms like virtual labs, simulation software, and online repositories of crosscurricular lesson plans are just a few examples of how technology can bolster crossdisciplinary learning. By incorporating these tools, educators can create enriched learning environments that cater to diverse learning styles and preferences. Project-Based Learning: A Cross-Disciplinary Gold Mine Project-based learning (PBL) is an educational approach that organizes learning around projects. According to the Buck Institute for Education, PBL involves students in long-term, collaborative projects that integrate various subject areas and are both curriculum-based and real-world oriented. It encourages learners to investigate problems, propose solutions, and work with peers to create tangible, public products. On November 5, 2023, Green View Elementary introduced a school-wide PBL initiative focusing on environmental sustainability. By integrating science, social studies, mathematics, and language arts, the program allowed students to research renewable energy, calculate potential savings, and present proposals to the local community. Maria Gonzalez, the school's PBL coordinator, noted, "Students are not just learning facts; they are becoming problem-solvers and change-makers in our community." Collaborative Learning Spaces The design and structure of the learning environment is also a key component of successful cross-disciplinary teaching. Modern classrooms are increasingly shifting away from traditional rows of desks to more collaborative learning spaces that encourage teamwork and idea-sharing among students. In line with this trend, Franklin Middle School redesigned its classrooms in September 2023 to feature mobile workstations and communal areas where students can work together on cross-curricular projects. Daniel Levinson, Franklin's principal, reported, "Since the shift, we've seen an uptick in student participation, peer-to-peer tutoring, and a sense of community in our classrooms." Training Educators for Cross-Disciplinary Facilitation For cross-disciplinary methodologies to be effectively implemented, teachers themselves need to be proficient in integrating curriculum content and adept at facilitating student-led learning. Professional development programs are crucial in equipping educators with the required skills. The Jackson District initiated a professional development series on September 20, 2023, where teachers received training in collaborative teaching strategies and cross-curricular planning. Evelyn Richards, a teacher who participated in the series, shared, "This training has transformed my approach to teaching. I'm now able to design lessons that not only cover multiple subject areas but also engage students in deeper learning." Assessment Strategies for Cross-Disciplinary Education In cross-disciplinary education, traditional testing methods may not fully capture the essence of what students are learning. Alternative assessment techniques are, therefore, increasingly important. Portfolios, peer reviews, and reflective journals are some methods that provide a more comprehensive picture of student progress in a cross-disciplinary environment. Linda Hart, an educational researcher, presented a report on January 10, 2024, suggesting that "assessment in a cross-disciplinary context should be as multi-faceted as the learning itself. It should evaluate not just content knowledge but also skills like critical thinking and teamwork." Building Partnerships with the Community Schools do not operate in isolation and can benefit from the resources and expertise found in their local communities. Partnerships with businesses, nonprofit organizations, and higher education institutions can enhance the breadth and depth of cross-disciplinary learning experiences. Eastgate Academy partnered with a local tech startup on February 24, 2024, to provide students with the opportunity to work on real software development projects, incorporating elements of computer science, mathematics, and business studies. Principal Jennifer Smith explained, "Our collaboration with community partners exposes students to real-life applications of their learning and valuable mentorship opportunities." Engaging Parents in Cross-Disciplinary Initiatives Parents play a significant role in supporting and reinforcing cross-disciplinary learning at home. Schools can foster parent engagement by holding informational sessions, offering resources, and involving them in cross-curricular events. On March 8, 2024, Oakwood Secondary held a 'Family Science Night' where parents participated in experiments alongside their children, integrating scientific inquiry with family bonding. Tom Weber, the school's community liaison officer, remarked, "Engaging families in our cross-disciplinary activities strengthens our educational community and extends learning beyond the classroom walls." Looking Forward: The Promise of Cross-Disciplinary Education As schools around the globe increasingly embrace innovative teaching methodologies, the promise of a more integrated, relevant, and engaging education system comes into view. Cross-disciplinary approaches are at the forefront of this educational revolution, offering a more holistic learning experience that mirrors the complexity of the world students will enter upon graduation. By adopting these practices, schools can equip learners with the necessary skills to thrive in an ever-changing landscape.
required_spans: Cross-disciplinary learning dismantles the walls between different subjects
Project-based learning (PBL) is an educational approach that organizes learning around projects
Portfolios, peer reviews, and reflective journals are some methods that provide a more comprehensive picture of student progress in a cross-disciplinary environment

text:
# Small and Medium Enterprises

Internet of Things (IoT): Revolutionizing SME Supply Chain Management In the ever-evolving landscape of global business, Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) are increasingly turning to technology to gain a competitive edge. Among the most transformative advancements is the integration of the Internet of Things (IoT) into supply chain management, an innovation that is redefining efficiency, accuracy, and responsiveness in remarkable ways. The IoT Supply Chain: A New Frontier for SMEs The supply chain is the heartbeat of any business, and for SMEs, robust and real-time supply chain management is no longer a luxury but a necessity. IoT technology has emerged as a game-changer, enabling these businesses to track assets, manage inventory, and respond to market changes with agility and insight that were once the domain of only the largest corporations. Since September 2023, there has been an upsurge in SMEs adopting IoT solutions thanks to the technology becoming more accessible and cost-effective. With IoT, every item, vehicle, and piece of equipment can be interconnected, offering real-time data that can be used to optimize processes and decision-making. Case Study: From Obscurity to Market Leader Consider the success story of 'EverFresh Produce', a mid-sized supplier of organic fruits and vegetables based out of Portland, Maine. Founder Johnathan Meyers recalls the struggles before adopting an IoT-based supply chain system in late 2023. "We were playing catch-up to demand surges and our waste levels were intolerable," he says. After implementing an array of sensors to track their products from farm to market and real-time data analytics, they reduced waste by 30% and improved delivery times by 50%. Sensor Technology: The Eyes and Ears of an IoT Supply Chain The IoT ecosystem is fundamentally built on sensors that collect data on everything from temperature to location to speed. For instance, RFID tags can offer instant information on an item's whereabouts, turning inventory management from an educated guess into a precise science. In the case of 'EverFresh Produce', sensors are placed in packaging that detects and records temperature to ensure optimal conditions for perishable goods during transit. These sensors communicate with a central system that alerts if any produce is at risk of spoiling, allowing for immediate action which has vastly improved their reputation for quality. Real-Time Analytics and AI: Turning Data into Action Harnessing the power of IoT requires sophisticated analytics to make sense of the data collected. Cloud computing and AI have empowered SMEs like 'EverFresh Produce' with predictive insights, helping them anticipate supply chain bottlenecks before they become disruptive. This move toward predictive analytics was markedly advanced when small business owner Anita Rodriguez launched her IoT-driven logistics company 'TrackWise Solutions' in January 2024. Her platform offers SMEs the ability to integrate IoT data with machine learning algorithms for dynamic routing, inventory forecasting, and even predictive maintenance for machinery and transport vehicles. IoT and Customer Satisfaction: Closing the Loop SMEs are also leveraging IoT to elevate the customer experience by providing more transparency and consistency. 'Quantum Crafts', a custom furniture manufacturer from Richmond, Virginia, adopted an IoT system that allows customers to see the progress of their orders in real-time. CEO Marcus Washington notes, "Since launching our IoT tracking system in April 2024, customer satisfaction scores have skyrocketed. They feel more connected to the product and value the transparency." Building Resilience in Supply Chains with IoT Resilience has become a watchword in supply chain management, especially following disruptions like those experienced globally during the early 2020s. IoT technology has afforded SMEs like 'Global Textiles Inc.', a family-owned fabric distributor in Austin, Texas, the tools to develop a responsive and adaptive supply chain. In November 2023, when a container ship carrying their goods was delayed due to bad weather, IoT-enabled monitoring allowed 'Global Textiles Inc.' to not only notify customers of the delay but also rearrange logistics to minimize the impact. The result was a boost in trust and loyalty from their client base. Interconnectivity and Collaboration: The IoT Ecosystem The true potential of IoT in supply chain management for SMEs lies in the creation of interconnected ecosystems that extend beyond the confines of a single enterprise. Collaborative platforms can link producers, suppliers, distributors, and even customers, fostering a synergy that optimizes the entire supply chain. In September 2024, a consortium of small businesses, including 'EverFresh Produce', 'Quantum Crafts', and several others joined forces to create a shared IoT platform called 'ChainLink Ecosystem'. By sharing data and insights, they have opened up new opportunities for efficiency, including collective bargaining with transport and logistics vendors, reducing individual costs through synergy. Investing in IoT: Challenges and Opportunities for SMEs For SMEs, the journey towards a fully integrated IoT supply chain is not without its hurdles. Investment in technology, concerns about cybersecurity, and the need for employee training present challenges. However, the opportunities for growth, efficiency, and customer satisfaction are persuasive incentives. Entities like 'Small Biz Tech', an innovation center launched in February 2025, offer resources, funding assistance, and expertise to SMEs looking to make the transition to IoTbased operations. Its founder, Rachel Thompson, believes "IoT is not just a trend; it's the future of supply chain management. SMEs that recognize and embrace this stand to gain significantly in a competitive marketplace." Green Supply Chain: IoT for Sustainability Environmental responsibility is another domain where IoT is making significant headways for SME supply chains. Monitoring resources and optimizing routes not only cuts costs but also reduces carbon footprints. 'EcoWare Packaging', a startup specializing in sustainable packaging, uses IoT sensors to manage its raw material use more effectively, and as of its first quarter in 2025, has achieved a 20% decrease in energy consumption. Harnessing the Momentum of IoT As the Internet of Things continues to advance, SMEs are discovering the immense benefit that comes from adopting this technology for supply chain management. Every new success story adds to the momentum, encouraging further exploration of IoT's virtually limitless potential for transforming businesses. With customized solutions increasingly available, it's an exciting time for SMEs to consider how IoT could revolutionize their own supply chain processes. From increased efficiency and reduced waste to enhanced customer satisfaction and sustainability, the IoT is a catalyst for small and medium-sized enterprises aiming to scale up and compete on a global stage.
required_spans: IoT technology has emerged as a game-changer, enabling these businesses to track assets, manage inventory, and respond to market changes with agility
RFID tags can offer instant information on an item's whereabouts, turning inventory management from an educated guess into a precise science
Cloud computing and AI have empowered SMEs like 'EverFresh Produce' with predictive insights

text:
# Local Education Systems

Bridging the Gap: How Community Learning Centers Enhance Access to Education In cities and neighborhoods across the globe, the chasm between the availability of quality education and community needs has been markedly vast. Yet, in recent years, a beacon of hope has emerged in the form of Community Learning Centers (CLCs), institutions dedicated to offering educational resources and support to individuals regardless of their ages, backgrounds, or financial statuses. This article delves into how CLCs are revolutionizing learning and offering a lifeline to those who have been left behind by traditional educational systems. A New Dawn for Learning In the heart of Johannesburg, South Africa, a stirring narrative of transformation is unfolding. On October 5, 2023, the Soweto Community Learning Center opened its doors, symbolizing a new era for the region's disenfranchised youth. Founded by educational entrepreneur Naledi Mokoena and a group of local philanthropists, the center has become a hive of activity and a pioneer in communal education. At Soweto CLC, the mornings buzz with children participating in literacy and numeracy classes, while afternoons and evenings welcome adults who are either finishing their secondary education or engaging in vocational training. By October 15, over 300 community members had enrolled, with that number steadily rising. Naledi observes, "The hunger for learning here is palpable; the CLC is not just a place of learning, but a sanctuary where dreams are nurtured." Empowerment Through Education Halfway across the world in Santa Monica, USA, a similar story unfolds. Since its inauguration on September 17, 2023, the Ocean Park Community Learning Center has become a fulcrum around which the local community rallies. Director John Davis and his team offer a broad range of programs including GED preparation, English as a Second Language (ESL) courses, and computer literacy workshops. One standout program is the "Second Chance" initiative, which supports individuals transitioning from incarceration back into society. The success stories are heartwarming – with one such tale belonging to Maria Rodriguez, who after enrolling in Ocean Park CLC's entrepreneurial courses, launched her own catering business in January 2024. Maria says, "The center gave me the tools and confidence to rebuild my life. Learning here feels like a family; we lift each other up." Fostering Community Ties Through Education In Asia, the Tokyo Metropolitan Government took a commendable step by inaugurating the Shinjuku Community Learning Center on November 2, 2023. Spearheaded by community leader Hiroshi Takahashi, the center notably emphasizes not just individual education, but also community building. By fostering a sense of camaraderie and collective learning, the center has become an indispensable asset in the locality. The Shinjuku CLC orchestrates community festivals, where the successes of its members are celebrated. During one such event on December 12, 2023, an interactive exhibit showcased the work of adult learners in various craft and technical skills learned within the center. This public celebration not only highlighted the center's accomplishments but also helped to dissolve barriers between different segments of society, fostering inclusiveness and unity. When Learning Knows No Age The typical educational paradigm often associates learning with the young, but CLCs across the globe actively challenge this notion. In the scenic town of Queenstown, New Zealand, seniors are finding a zest for life at the Remarkables CLC, introduced on September 25, 2023. The center's focus on lifelong learning has attracted retirees like 70-year-old Douglas Taylor, who is now an avid student in the digital literacy program. "Learning doesn't stop because you've reached a certain age," Douglas remarks with a chuckle. "I'm not just learning to use technology; I'm staying relevant and connected with my grandchildren." Remarkables CLC has created an inclusive environment that diminishes the digital divide and provides a sense of purpose and community for the older generation. The Power of Tailored Programs Customization is key in the successful approach of CLCs, as they tailor programs to meet the specific needs of their communities. This fact is exemplified by the East London CLC in the United Kingdom, which was established on September 30, 2023, in an area with a high percentage of immigrant families. Director Aisha Yusuf, herself an immigrant, understood the unique challenges these families faced. Under her guidance, the center now offers bespoke programs that include language support, cultural orientation workshops, and legal aid services. The tailored programs have a profound impact. Fifteen-year-old Amina, who migrated from Syria with her family, now excels in school thanks to the supplemental tutoring and language assistance she received at East London CLC. "They didn't just teach me English," Amina reflects, "they helped me find my voice in a new world." The Synergy of Partnership Community Learning Centers often thrive on collaboration with local businesses, governments, and non-profits. In Rio de Janeiro's favelas, the vibrant Favela Educarte CLC launched on October 11, 2023, thanks to a partnership between local authorities, international NGOs, and the private sector. Their joint efforts have resulted in a center that offers not just education but also nutrition programs and healthcare to the children attending. This holistic approach has turned the center into a lifeline for the community. Carlos Henrique, a small business owner from the neighborhood, volunteers as a mentor, inspired by the comprehensive support the center extends. His mentorship has led several teenagers to internships and even employment within his enterprise. The ripple effect of these partnerships underscores the multifaceted benefits of community-centric education. Embracing the Digital Future In the digital age, CLCs are not being left behind. Innovative measures to integrate technology into the learning experience have been taken by the Seoul Digital CLC, which opened its state-of-the-art facility on September 12, 2023. Boasting virtual classrooms and online learning platforms, the center extends its reach beyond physical boundaries, allowing for remote learning opportunities that are crucial in the modern world. Ji-hoon, a software developer who dedicates part of his time to teaching coding at the center, shares, "It's amazing to see people from all walks of life, and even from remote areas, learning to code. We are not just educating; we are opening doors to new futures." Charting Success in Every Step The success of Community Learning Centers cannot be measured solely in numbers and statistics. Each individual who walks through their doors and finds hope, each life altered by the power of education, each community transformed by the commitment to learning – these are the true indicators of success. By bringing learning into the heart of communities and making it accessible to all, CLCs are indeed bridging the educational gap on an unprecedented scale. With innovative practices, diverse program offerings, and a spirit of communal uplift, these centers exemplify the adage that education is the most powerful weapon which can be used to change the world. As we look to the future, we can take heart in the knowledge that through the collective efforts of CLCs around the globe, education is becoming increasingly accessible, inclusive, and transformative. It's a testament to the enduring power of learning and the indomitable spirit of communities united by the pursuit of knowledge.
required_spans: Soweto Community Learning Center opened its doors, symbolizing a new era
Ocean Park Community Learning Center has become a fulcrum around which the local community rallies

text:
# News Stories

Navigating Uncharted Waters: The Rise of Virtual Economies In an unprecedented economic phenomenon, the virtual dimension has begun overshadowing the tangible markets we've long been accustomed to. This cognitive dissonance within the realm of economics is the result of the recent effervescence of virtual economies, managing to become an integral yet nearly ethereal component of our financial ecosystem. A Paradigm Shift Post September 1, 2023 Since September 1, 2023, analysts and economists like Juliana Chen and Michael Oluwakemi turned their focus towards the rapid growth and the formidably complex interdependencies emerging within virtual economies. They observed that within these realms, everything from cryptocurrency to in-game assets, culminating in substantial real-world economic impact. As we come to terms with phenomena like virtual real estate selling for millions and avatars generating income that rivals real-world jobs, it becomes evident that this new economic frontier is redefining wealth creation, employment, and investment. The Concept of Non-Physical Assets One must wonder, what are the ways economic value could be derived from assets that are neither tangible nor traditional? We live in a world where kids trading virtual cards in online games are making smarter investment decisions than some seasoned stockbrokers. Here lies the quintessence of virtual economies that thrive solely on the demand and supply of digital goods and services – a marketplace once considered inconsequential. Virtual economies are characterized by in-game currencies, digital assets, and NFTs (Non-Fungible Tokens), which have all seen skyrocketing interest post-September 2023. The Intrinsic Value Debate The first bone of contention arises when considering the intrinsic value of virtual goods. Critics like esteemed economist Laura Ingram argue that these assets lack any real intrinsic value—being built on a fragile foundation of consumer faith much like the Tulip Mania of the 17th century. However, proponents like tech entrepreneur Jason Amir counter that the intrinsic value is in the eye of the beholder, noting that even fiat currency is ultimately a social construct based on consensus rather than any actual, physical backing since leaving the gold standard. The Regulatory Wild West October 17, 2023, marked the date when the lack of regulation in these virtual spaces became glaringly apparent. Massive hackings, scams, and the volatility of in-game currencies caused financial loss to many. Philip Tan, a professor of Game Design at MIT, emphasizes that "in the absence of clear regulations, virtual economies have become the frontiers of money laundering and other nefarious financial activities." Yet despite this, global regulations remain few and far between, leading to the 'Wild West' comparison. Employment or Exploitation? With new economic avenues come new forms of labor. There are now full-time 'gold farmers' in online games, virtual real estate agents, and digital asset creators. A bold declaration was made by United Nations' virtual labor specialist, Sofia Ramirez, on December 2, 2023, stating that "Virtual economies are creating employment opportunities, but also spheres where exploitation can go unchecked." The ethical debate hinges on virtual sweatshops where workers earn less than their physical-world counterparts for comparable labor, under unregulated conditions. Social and Cultural Implications Beyond economics, virtual economies challenge social constructs. Families eating dinner together while a teenager is mentally clocked into a virtual world, trading resources, signifies a cultural shift. Renowned sociologist Dr. Alexei Petrov spoke on November 29, 2023, about the potential for virtual economies to "reshape how we perceive work-life balance, social interactions, and even our own identities." Environmental and Energy Concerns Despite their non-physical nature, there is an undeniable environmental footprint associated with the rise of virtual economies. The massive energy consumption necessary to maintain servers, execute digital transactions, and mine cryptocurrencies have environmentalists like Emma Rocha raising alarms about the sustainability of these platforms. A virtual economy may not pollute in the traditional sense, but its carbon footprint is real. Wealth Inequality and Market Access The introduction of virtual economies doesn't necessarily translate into equitable market access. On January 5, 2024, activist David Kwong highlighted how "Global wealth inequality can be perpetuated and amplified within virtual economies, where financial prowess in the real world often translates directly to virtual dominance." Consequently, this raises questions about the potential of virtual economies to democratize opportunity or merely mirror and exacerbate existing disparities. Hyper-Real Economies and Mental Health The existential challenge posed by virtual economies is how they are affecting our mental health and perceptions of value. Psychologist Dr. Nina Patel warned on February 14, 2024, of the "psychological risks of hyper-real economies that blur the lines between reality and simulation." The addictive nature of these economies and the virtual fulfillment they offer pose severe concerns for mental well-being. Future Prospects and Preparations Venturing into 2024, preparing for the burgeoning virtual economy becomes paramount. Educational institutions have begun integrating virtual economic literacy into curricula, and financial advisors are increasingly specializing in digital asset management. Governments and regulators are under pressure to develop frameworks that ensure these markets are equitable, transparent, and secure. International bodies are beginning to convene to discuss the global impacts of these virtual spaces. The debate continues as to how society will navigate the intricacies of virtual economies. Will we categorically dismiss them as fanciful, intangible creations unworthy of attention, or will we adapt our ethical, regulatory, and economic frameworks to embrace them fully? What is agreed upon is that the rise of virtual economies is a remarkable display of human innovation that is as disruptive as it is seductive—a phenomenon of the 21st century that we cannot afford to ignore.
required_spans: virtual real estate selling for millions

text:
# VR&E Reemployment Track | Veterans Affairs#1

VR&E Reemployment track As a Veteran , you re protected under the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act USERRA. This means you can t be disadvantaged in your civilian career because of your service. If you re a Veteran with a service - connected disability , the Vocational Rehabilitation and Employment VR&E Reemployment track can help your employer accommodate your needs. Your Vocational Rehabilitation Counselor VRC can provide a full range of rehabilitation services, and can refer you directly to the Department of Labor to begin the process. Find out how we can help you return to your former job and support your employer in meeting your needs. Can I get help returning to my former job through VR&E? You may be eligible for help if you re a Veteran with a service - connected disability , and you meet all of the requirements listed below. All of these must be true. You: Have an employment barrier or handicap , and Are enrolled in Vocational Rehabilitation and Employment VR&E , and Would like to return to your former job Note: Having an employment handicap means your service - connected disability limits your ability to prepare for, obtain, and maintain suitable employment a job that doesn t make your disability worse, is stable, and matches your abilities, aptitudes, and interests. What kind of benefits can I get? Help with the process of returning to the job you held before you deployed. How do I get these benefits? First , you ll need to apply for VR&E benefits. Then , you ll work with your Vocational Rehabilitation Counselor VRC. If you have your VA disability rating, follow these steps to apply: Sign in to your eBenefits account. Click Additional Benefits on your dashboard. Choose Vocational Rehabilitation and Employment Program. Apply for the Vocational Rehabilitation and Employment program. If you re eligible , we ll invite you to an orientation session at your nearest VA regional benefit office. Ready to apply? Go to eBenefits to apply Find out how to apply if you haven t yet received a disability rating Get more information We offer opportunities to get training and practical hands - on work experience at the same time through programs like : The VR&E Special Employer Incentives SEI program for eligible Veterans who face challenges getting a job. Download the SEI program fact sheet PDF The VR&E Non - Paid Work Experience NPWE program for eligible Veterans and service members who have an established career goal and learn easily in a hands - on environment or are having trouble getting a job due to lack of work experience. Download the NPWE program fact sheet PDF Watch this video to learn more about the NPWE program We also offer : VR&E employment coordinators located at our VA regional offices and many of our out - based locations who can help you access the employment services available to you. Find an employment coordinator near you Counselors to help you transition from military to college life. Learn about VetSuccess on Campus Links to more resources outside VA that can help you in your job search. See our list of external resources If you re participating in a VR&E employment program and lost your housing because of a natural or other disaster , you may qualify for 2 more months of the Employment Adjustment Allowance. Contact your VR&E counselor for more information
required_spans: Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act USERRA

text:
# Cybersecurity News

AI and Machine Learning in Cybersecurity: Stepping Up Our Game As the sun dips below the digital horizon, casting long shadows across a world brimming with data, an army of cyber guardians keep watch. Amid these sentinels stands an emerging technological prowess: artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) cybersecurity technologies. Dr. Evelyn Chase of Chicago, one of the premier cybersecurity experts worldwide has harnessed these resources in her defense against digital threats. From Concept to Application: The Digital Watchguard Begins its Journey As we progress further into the digital era, traditional cybersecurity measures no longer suffice. Dr. Chase addressed an audience of IT experts at the Chicago Cyber Security Summit on October 15th 2023 and described digital landscape as a battlefield where complexity was weaponized - here she presented groundbreaking work integrating AI with cybersecurity. Dr. Chase emphasizes the power of AI and ML to analyze, learn, and respond effectively to cyber threats. Unlike static defense mechanisms, AI-powered security systems evolve by learning from past attacks to plug gaps and predict potential breaches before they take place. Foundations of Machine Learning: Informing the Custodial Code Dr. Chase's research demonstrates the core of AI cybersecurity is machine learning. Her team utilizes machine learning algorithms to train systems on massive datasets that include examples of malware, ransomware and other forms of intrusions; over time and exposure, these systems become adept at distinguishing benign from malicious activity while further sharpening their judgement skills to become razor-sharp. On December 1st 2023, Dr. Chase demonstrated how Machine Learning algorithms had successfully detected and quarantined an elaborate phishing attempt that threatened Chicago City Council infrastructure. These defense mechanisms provide a flexible response to cybercriminal tactics by monitoring patterns and anomalies within data. Real-Time Attack Reflexes of AI At the core of Dr. Chase's framework lies real-time threat detection using artificial intelligence systems with neural networks and advanced machine learning models. His models were instrumental in quickly detecting an attempt at zero-day vulnerability exploit against a financial institution in Chicago in November 2023, which would otherwise have resulted in catastrophic data breach if left undetected by human operators. AI systems excel at quickly responding to modern cybersecurity threats where delays could spell disaster. AI systems are adept at sorting through everyday network traffic to detect true threats - often finding that insidious needle in the digital haystack! Harmonization of Technologies Through Algorithms Dr. Chase has proven the value of integrating AI with other emerging technologies to strengthen our digital defenses, such as blockchain for immutable ledgers of transactions, quantum computing for rapid decryption and encryption and IoT for comprehensive device protection - these elements combine perfectly with AI to form a comprehensive cybersecurity toolkit. On January 3rd 2024, Dr. Chase unveiled his project combining AI with encrypted blockchain technology to provide secure transactions and processes while being transparent and tamper-proof - providing industries struggling with trust issues an invaluable solution. Bridging the Gap Between Human and AI for Collaborative Defense Solutions Dr. Chase emphasizes the essentiality of human oversight amid technological marvels, reminding attendees at a webinar series for cybersecurity professionals on February 20, 2024 that artificial intelligence (AI) should only ever be seen as a tool and not as a replacement. Collaborative defense remains essential - while AI may execute various tasks efficiently, the nuanced judgment and ethical considerations still belong solely with humans. Dr. Chase's cybersecurity teams work hand in hand with AI systems, using insights and alerts gathered by AI systems to inform strategic decisions. Through an ongoing feedback loop, human experts and AI systems learn from each other, building an ever-strengthening defense against digital criminals. Cyber Guardian: Predictive Defense Strategies Predictive analytics present exciting possibilities in cybersecurity. AI's ability to recognize patterns while anticipating threats makes for preemptive defense strategies; Dr. Chase's work involves building predictive models using global cyber incident reports as input in order to predict attack vectors and protect organizations against future breaches. Her team is currently developing an initiative, set for launch by late 2024, that uses artificial intelligence (AI) to simulate potential attacks using predictive elements - creating virtual war games in cybersecurity terms - in order to allow organizations to prepare themselves against and neutralize potential threats before they develop into actual real-world attacks. Balance of Intelligence: AI's Ethics in Cybersecurity Dr. Chase remains mindful of AI's potential, yet never loses sight of its ethical ramifications. How can we ensure AI's power is used responsibly and respectably in regard to privacy and personal data protection? Dr. Chase devotes much of her research time pondering these matters. Her AI ethics charter, widely respected in cybersecurity circles, serves as a blueprint for responsible deployment of artificial intelligence technologies. The document emphasizes transparency, accountability, and respect for privacy norms - creating a blueprint for how AI should be utilized against cybercrime. Mindmeld of Man and Machine: Human Element in AI Dr. Chase's journey in integrating AI and ML into cybersecurity reveals a consistent theme: the combination of human intuition with machine intelligence is the most potent defense against cyber threats. Her vision paves a path for industry professionals by showing that not just technology alone creates impregnable digital domains. Dr. Evelyn Chase's Foresight Dr. Chase remains focused on her mission despite all the accolades that come her way, remaining true to herself in every sense. Her dedication and brilliance serve as an example of strategic evolution through AI/ML in cybersecurity as she deftly navigates its murky waters with dedication and brilliance. Her journey through AI/ML cybersecurity stands as a beacon in an otherwise dim future landscape; her journey provides guidance in our digital history.

# Adding and removing users#3

When DMV creates a LENS roster , we issue a personal user ID and password to the applicant. If your organization has more than one person who may use LENS , we will gladly issue additional user IDs. Every LENS user may, and should, have their own personal logon. Please do not share your logon or password with others. How do I add a user? To get an additional user ID , please email us at [email protected] [2] with all of the following your 5-digit LENS roster number the user s NY State Driver license / non - driver ID number 1 the user's full name the business name the user's business address the user s business telephone number the user s business email address We will then send the user ID to the user s business email address, and mail the password to the user s personal license / ID mailing address. The password is the only LENS information that we ever send to a user s personal mailing address. We will not send a password to an office address. How do I remove a user? If a LENS user leaves your organization you can remove them by completing the following steps : log on to LENS visit the LENS Main Menu and select Customer Information select User Profile Maintenance click Search to list all of your LENS users find the departing user and check the corresponding Remove box. Click Process. If a LENS user leaves your organization and you cannot log in to LENS , please email us at [email protected] [2]. Identify the user and your LENS customer number. We will disable the departed user s access to your LENS roster. How do I change users? If someone is taking on another user s duties , follow the instructions in the topic above for requesting additional user IDs. Do not share the former user s ID and password with the new user. We will not provide support to any person who is using another person s user ID. We may revoke shared user IDs upon discovery. Register for LENS [3 ] Log in to LENS [1 ] If the license / ID is from another state , send : state, name, address, ID number and expiration date.
